Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Compiler warning/error or silent wrong answer due to header files being missing?from rtw_shared_utils.h
Description: The code generator might omit required header files from the generated file rtw_shared_utils.h .?This can occur, under rare circumstances, if you generate code and all the following conditions apply: You did one of the following:Generated code for a?top model that contains at least?three Model blocks (referenced models). Consecutively generated code for at least?three top models that use the same shared utility location (Code Generation>Interface>Shared code placement  is set to Shared location ).?   The generated code for these models includes different header files. The code generator produces at least one of the following: Different user-defined data type files (for example, Simulink.AliasTypes  with the?DataScope  property?set to Exported ) Different reusable library subsystem files in the shared utility folder _sharedutils    ?The missing header files can result in one of the following: Compiler warning Compiler error Executable program that produces a silent wrong answer
Workaround: If you are generating code for a top model that contains at least three?Model blocks do both of the following:Set?the DataScope on the datatype objects to Auto. This prevents the code generator from producing data type files in?the _sharedutils folder.? Disable library links on subsystems used in the models before generating code. This prevents the code generator from producing reusable library subsystem files?in?the _sharedutils folder.If you are?consecutively generating code for at least three top?models that use the same shared utility code, do one of the following:Set the model configuration parameter Code Generation>Interface>Shared code placement to Auto.?Do both of the following:Set?the?DataScope on the datatype objects to Auto. This prevents the code generator from producing data type files in?the _sharedutils folder.Disable library links on subsystems used in the models before generating code. This prevents the code generator from producing reusable library subsystem files?in?the _sharedutils folder.

Summary: Simulink Web View might generate corrupt SVG files
Description: When a model has blocks with long non-ASCII parameter values, Simulink Web View incorrectly truncates these strings when generating block parameters tables. Truncating the strings might result in corrupt SVG files.
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.  Exit MATLAB.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.      Windows Systems: You may need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To do this, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode, then from the browser save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.     For each file that appears in the .zip file, find the corresponding file in your existing <MATLAB> folder and make a backup of it.  To see the contents of the .zip file:          Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.      Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):        unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>         Extract the files from the .zip file into the <MATLAB> folder.          Windows Systems:                  Open the .zip file.          Extract the contents of the file into your <MATLAB> folder. You may be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when extracting the .zip file.                    Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):         cd <MATLAB>        unzip <.zip file>         Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Interrupts can cause model signals to become NaN (not a number)
Description: When a real-time executable runs on some target computers, signals can become NaN when a CPU interrupt occurs. This problem has been observed on target computers with Intel Core 2 Duo or Intel Pentium CPUs.
Workaround: Filter out the NaN in the model by calling a function such as isnan.

Summary: Cross-compiler workflow for protected models can give an nmake error
Description: Simulating a protected model created with Visual Studio on a computer that does not have Visual Studio installed generates an nmake  error. The specified configuration type is missing.  The tools for the configuration might not be installed. 'nmake' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. The make command returned an error of 9009 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ?
Workaround: Install Visual Studio on the computer used to simulate the protected model.

Summary: Incorrect MISRA-C 2004 rule 12.5 violation when accessing structure fields in operands of logical operators
Description: If a structure field is accessed using the arrow operator (-> ) or the dot operator (. ), and you use this access expression as the operand of a logical operator, then an incorrect MISRA-C 2004 12.5 violation may be produced.  This example produces the incorrect 12.5 violation: typedef unsigned char boolean; typedef struct {    boolean myMember1;   boolean myMember2; } myStruct; extern boolean var; boolean foo(myStruct *tmp) {    boolean res;   res = var && (tmp->myMember2); /* incorrect MISRA 2004 12.5 violation */    return res;}
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds. First, you can use an intermediate variable to hold the value of the accessed field:typedef unsigned char boolean; typedef struct {    boolean myMember1;   boolean myMember2; } myStruct; extern boolean var; boolean foo(myStruct *tmp) {    boolean res;   boolean tvar;   tvar = (tmp->myMember2);   res = var && tvar; /* no MISRA 2004 12.5 violation */    return res;}Second you can add an explicit test against 0:typedef unsigned char boolean; typedef struct {    boolean myMember1;   boolean myMember2; } myStruct; extern boolean var; boolean foo(myStruct *tmp) {    boolean res;   res = var && (tmp->myMember2 != 0); /* no MISRA 2004 12.5 violation */    return res;?

Summary: Build process failure with error Function inlining failed for model with data type mismatch in Constant block
Description: An internal assertion may occur when both of the following are true: A Constant block refers to a Simulink.Parameter  object with DataType  set to single . For the same Constant block, in the Source Block Parameters dialog box, on the Signal Attributes  pane, the Output data type  is set to double.
Workaround: Specify matching data types for the Simulink.Parameter object and the Output data type.?

Summary: Derived range analysis using the Fixed-Point Tool hangs when model contains large matrices
Description: When a model contains either signals or parameters that exceed 250,000 elements, the derived range process will hang indefinitely. You must exit MATLAB to stop the process.

Summary: EPS files created from Simulink models are large and low quality
Description: When printing a model to an encapsulated postscript file using the print  command or Simulink print dialog box, the generated EPS file size is much larger than those made by previous releases of Simulink.  At least some portion of the image is a rasterized bitmap instead of vector graphics, resulting in poor image quality.
Workaround: Use a different vector graphics file format, such as SVG. You can also use a third-party image converter to make EPS files from the SVG files created by Simulink.

Summary: Floating point to integer casts in Stateflow Charts using C Action Language can produce incorrect derived range analysis results
Description: In some cases, floating point to integer casts in Stateflow Charts using C Action Language can produce incorrect derived range analysis results.  The following conditions must be true to trigger this bug: The Stateflow Chart uses C Action Language The chart has 'Saturate on integer overflow' disabled The chart must contain floating point variables or expressions that are cast to an integer type Either the initial condition or all possible values of the floating point variable or expression that is being cast are outside the range of the cast output integer type  The range of a 32 bit signed integer value is -2^31 to 2^31-1. A floating point variable or expression with data type double and an initial condition value of 2^32 triggers this bug if it is cast to an int32 type. Triggering this bug results in missing range results for model entities where they are expected. In some cases the calculated ranges could be imprecise or too small.

Summary: Training a pattern recognition neural network (patternnet) with gpuArrays can generate a CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_FAILED error
Description: A CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_FAILED  error can occur when training a patternnet  using gpuArrays with a dataset above a certain size. Specifically, it is the size of the input vectors that matters, not the number of training examples. It does not occur if the dimension of the input vectors is reduced to 40.
Workaround: After the error occurs, reset the GPU using the command reset(gpuDevice). Otherwise, all subsequent GPU commands will error.Subsequently, to avoid this error, do not manually convert the training data to gpuArray, that is, do not use nndata2gpu. Instead, specify the 'useGPU' option when you call train, as follows:net = train(net,x,t,'useGPU','yes');

Summary: The hyper link 'Waveform Design to Improve Performance of an Existing Radar System' in 'Stream and Accelerate Simulation of Radar System' example page doesn't work.
Description: When the customer clicks the 'Waveform Design to Improve Performance of an Existing Radar System' link in 'Stream and Accelerate Simulation of Radar System' (which can be accessed using the following command)  web([docroot '/phased/examples/stream-and-accelerate-simulation-of-radar-system.html'])  the link is broken

Summary: Two Lookup table blocks incorrectly share one utility function when input data types are heterogeneous and break point data types are homogeneous, all other parameters being identical.
Description: Two almost identical Lookup table blocks incorrectly share one utility function when: Breakpoint data types are homogeneous fixed-point data types Input port data types are heterogeneous fixed-point data types and different from their corresponding breakpoint data types All other parameters are identical  This bug occurs because when we name a utility function while using fixed-point data types, we calculate the difference of fraction between each input and it's corresponding breakpoint. Specially, if all the breakpoints are homogeneous and all the inputs are also homogeneous, then only one difference needs to be taken when naming the utility function. However, only the homogenity of breakpoint data types was verified in determining this optimization.
Workaround: To work around this problem, set the data type of the breakpoints to Inherit: Same as corresponding input. If you need to set a different data type to the breakpoints, then there is no work around.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generating S-functions for models or subsystems with long names might cause errors?
Description: This bug may trigger a MATLAB crash or generate incorrect code inside fixed-point multiplication and division utilities. All   the following conditions must be true to trigger this bug: An S-Function is generated for the model or subsystem Generating code for the model or subsystem results in fixed-point multiplication or division utility functions The model or subsystem name exceeds 27 characters in length
Workaround: Rename the model or subsystem to a name at most 27 characters in length.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler generated applications that use Instrument Control and Vehicle Network toolbox fail to run correctly
Description: When running a deployed application created using MATLAB Compiler  for R2013b that uses functionality from Instrument Control Toolbox  or Vehicle Network Toolbox  on Windows (32-bit or 64-bit) , the application may fail to run correctly even if required third-party drivers for the application are installed on the host machine.The error messages that may be displayed are indicative of drivers or adaptors not being found by the application. For example:Specified ADAPTOR was not found or could not be loaded.  Alternatively, a java exception may be thrown:Java exception occurred:java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/sun/jna/Library...
Workaround: Apply the patch available on this page before running the compiled application.This patch needs to be executed once on every computer that your compiled application is expected to be deployed to. Before executing the patch, the MATLAB Compiler Runtime needs to be installed on computers that will ultimately be used to execute your compiled application.Patch Instructions  Log in to an account on your computer with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB Compiler Runtime installation. An account with administrator privileges is needed.  Create a folder called MATLABPatch in a location where you have write permissions.  Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store it in the MATLABPatch folder.  Extract the files from the .zip file to the MATLABPatch folder.  Delete the .zip file.  Run the executable with administrative privileges.            Right click on the .exe and select "Run as administrator".

Summary: MATLAB code that connects to a database using ODBC fails when deployed
Description: MATLAB code that connects to a database using ODBC, such as: conn = database.ODBCConnection('QTOOLS_DEV','','') fails when deployed with MATLAB Compiler or Builder products.  The error occurs during runtime of the deployed component and is similar to: Undefined function or variable 'database.internal.ODBCConnectHandle'.Error in database.ODBCConnection (line 51)Error in tmwTest (line 3)MATLAB:UndefinedFunction
Workaround: On the development machine:Add%#function database.ODBCConnection%#function database.internal.getJdbcParamsat the top of your MATLAB code to be compiled, in the entry level function.  It is not necessary to include this in all files, just the entry file is fine.Recompile and redistribute your application.On the target machine:Copy the following files from the MATLAB installation location:matlabroot\bin\arch\dodbc.dllmatlabroot\bin\registry\dodbc.xmlinto the corresponding locations in the MCR installation:mcrroot\bin\arch\dodbc.dllmcrroot\bin\registry\dodbc.xmlwhere matlabroot is the MATLAB installation location, mcrroot is the MCR installation location, and arch is the architecture, such as win32 or win64.If your deployed component is running against a MATLAB installation as opposed to an MCR installation, there is no need to copy the above files as they are already located on the MATLAB installation.

Summary: Incorrect product-of-inertia sign in models imported from SolidWorks? assemblies
Description: In models imported from SolidWorks?  assemblies, the products of inertia may have the wrong sign. For example, a SolidWorks product of inertia I_xy = +2.00000e-2 kg*m^2 may translate into SimMechanics?  as I_xy = -2.00000e-2 kg*m^2. The error stems from differences between the product-of-inertia definitions in the two modeling platforms. The products of inertia are the off-diagonal elements of the inertia tensor, which in SimMechanics software are defined as the volume integrals: I_yz = -ˇŇ yz dm I_zx = -ˇŇ zx dm I_xy = -ˇŇ xy dm  By contrast, SolidWorks software defines the products of inertia without the negative sign in front of the mass integrals. During CAD import, SimMechanics software sets Inertia Type  in each Solid block dialog box to Custom and populates the Products of Inertia  vector [I_yz, I_zx, I_xy] using the original SolidWorks values. These values have the wrong sign for a SimMechanics model. During simulation, the incorrect product-of-inertia signs cause inaccuracies in model dynamics, particularly in models with 3-D rotation. The inaccuracies are present even if motion appears natural. However, they tend to be less significant in planar mechanisms and may be negligible if the products of inertia are much smaller than the moments of inertiaˇŞe.g., if bodies are highly symmetric with respect to their reference frames. This bug also affects the CAD import examples based on the following SimMechanics Import XML files: fourbar.xml sm_robot.xml stewart_platform.xml  The affected examples are: sm_import_four_bar.slx sm_import_stewart_platform.slx  The product-of-inertia bug has been fixed for SimMechanics 4.4 (release R2014a). The CAD import examples will be fixed in SimMechanics 4.5 (release R2014b).
Workaround: Manually change the product-of-inertia signs in each imported Solid block. The simplest approach is to add a minus sign in front of the imported product-of-inertia vector, i.e.:-[I_yz, I_zx, I_xy]For example, if the imported product-of-inertia vector is[2.50000e-2, 0.00000, -5.87500e-3],the correct product-of-inertia vector is-[2.50000e-2, 0.00000, -5.87500e-3].

Summary: Simulating a model with a Bias block cannot detect overflow when Reuse Local Block Outputs is checked
Description: If you have a Simulink model that satisfies the following conditions The model has a Bias block The checkbox Confuguration Parameters->Optimization->Signals and Parameters->Code generation->Reuse Local Block Outputs  is checked The error diagnostic Confuguration Parameters->Diagnostics->Data Validity->Signals->Detect overflow  is set to error   then the model does not error out on detecting overflow when you simulate it using the sim()  command in the command line.  In R2012b and before, it does not error on detecting overflow even when you click the Run  button or press Ctrl+T . However, for these two methods, it does show an error in R2013a and R2013b on detecting overflow.
Workaround: Uncheck Confuguration Parameters->Optimization->Signals and Parameters->Code generation->Reuse Local Block Outputs when simulating this model.

Summary: Missing or wrongly placed context menu in tables
Description: If you right-click on a table, the context menu does not appear or appears in the wrong place. This can occur in the Design Editor table view and CAGE tables such as tradeoffs and any calibration table. The problem is worse when you have a large table and scroll to a cell.

Summary: Dot product or Unary minus block do not error out on integer overflow
Description: When the parameter Configuration parameter -> Diagnostic ->Data Validity -> Signals -> Detect Overflow is set to Error , the Dot product or Unary minus block do not error when there is an integer overflow, and you can get incorrect output.
Workaround: Set the parameter Configuration parameter -> Diagnostic ->Data Validity -> Signals -> Detect Overflowis set to Warning. The Dot product and Unary minus blocks will warn when there is an integer overflow.

Summary: Static functions generated from variant subsystems are not wrapped in preprocessor conditions
Description: When a variant of a variant subsystem generates a static function, the generated code might fail to compile. In this case, the static function is not conditionally compiled, but it references data structures that are conditionally compiled. An example of a model element that can produce a static function is a Stateflow graphical function.

Description: After creating a Neural Network using the "CreateModel" function, if you then modify either of the properties "HiddenLayers" or "Neurons", then the model ignores the changes and keeps the default model.  When changing the "Neurons" property, the model might error if the "HiddenLayers" are now mismatched. Summary: Dragging Simulink blocks with bitmapped images as icons can sometimes cause a crash.
Description: Dragging or resizing Simulink blocks whose icons are generated from bitmapped images can occasionally cause a crash.

Summary: Loading an object that contains a custom EventData object can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you create a custom event.EventData class and store instances of that event.EventData subclass as properties of another object, then saving the outer object to a .MAT file and later loading that file can cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Set the ConstructOnLoad attribute for your subclass of the event.EventData subclass. ConstructOnLoad causes MATLAB to invoke the constructor of your event.EventData subclass when an instance is loaded from a .MAT file, which prevents the crash from occurring.

Summary: Warnings running Model Advisor check Check usage of Merge blocks without Fixed-Point Designer?
Description: If you run Model Advisor check Check usage of Merge blocks  without Fixed-Point Designer, the check reports the warning: Cannot complete Merge analysis:  Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.

Summary: improfile errors when used interactively to profile an image displayed in a non-default coordinate system
Description: improfile  errors when used interactively to profile an image displayed in a non-default coordinate system, specifically when the non-default coordinate system is specified as an N-length vector of X/Y positions. For example:  X = linspace(0,1,100);Y = X; imagesc(X,Y,imread('pout.tif')) improfile

Summary: LLA to ECEF Position block does not handle latitude values outside the +90 and -90 degree range
Description: Using the LLA to ECEF Position block with latitude input values outside the +90 and -90 degree range can lead to incorrect output values.
Workaround: As a workaround, before passing latitude values to this block, preprocess the input values to make sure that they are within the +90 and -90 degree range. For example, if a location is described as 100 degrees latitude and 10 degrees longitude, the equivalent position is 80 degrees latitude and -170 degrees longitude.

Summary: audioplayer, sound, and soundsc produce impulse noise at end of playback
Description: When playing audio on Linux? and Mac OS X?, audioplayer  produces a small impulse noise at the end of playback. This noise is more pronounced when playing low-intensity audio. The following code reproduces the issue:load handelap = audioplayer(y, Fs);play(ap);  The sound  and soundsc  functions also display this behavior.
Workaround: To avoid the impulse noise at the end of the playback, pad the output signal with zeros to ensure that the playback ends at a zero-crossing. For example:load handelif isa(y, 'uint8')   y = [y; 128*ones(500, 1)];else   y = [y; zeros(500, 1, class(y))];endap = audioplayer(y, Fs);play(ap)

Summary: When a target application with too small a sample time is downloaded to the target computer, the erroneous message "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 0 us" appears.
Description: When a target application with a sample time lower than 8 microseconds is downloaded to the target machine, the target screen displays "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 0 us". The message should read "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 8 us".
Workaround: Users can ignore the erroneous message, because the software behaves as expected:Under interrupt mode, when a model with sample time less than 8 microseconds is downloaded to the target machine, the target screen shows the message "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 0 us". The correct message should be "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 8 us". The kernel sets the model sample time to 8 microseconds, although the message is incorrect. Under polling mode, when a model with sample time less than 500 nanoseconds is downloaded to the target machine, the target screen shows the message "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 0 us". The correct message should be "Error: sample time cannot be smaller than 0.5 us". The kernel sets the model sample time to 500 nanoseconds, although the message is incorrect.

Summary: Model Advisor "Check default transition placement in Stateflow charts" does not report violations for model with only one chart
Description: When running the MAAB check, "Check default transition placement in Stateflow charts" (jc_0531), Model Advisor does not report violations if the model has only one chart.

Summary: Subsystem names with certain suffixes might cause build failures during code generation
Description: While generating code from a model, errors might occur if noninlined subsystems with the following suffixes clash with other subsystem names:  _Start, _initialize, _Init, _Update, _Deriv, _Projection, _ForcingFunction, _MassMatrix, _ZC, _Enable, _Disable, _FinalizeDims, _SetDims, _Term and _Const   This issue only happens when your model contains 2 subsystems, for example, where one is named Test  and the other is named Test_Start  (or any other suffix from above). In R2013a and later, TLC errors are reported when generating code. In R2012b and earlier, a linking error is reported while building the model
Workaround: Avoid naming Subsystems that have the above suffixes in the name. If a clash does occur, rename either one of the subsystems.A MATLAB function is attached that scans a given model for potential issues and returns the potentially clashing subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer when an Assignment block inside a For Iterator subsystem or a While iterator subsystem is on a conditional execution input branch
Description: Simulink will produce an incorrect answer (both in simulation and code generation) if all of the following three conditions are satisfied: The parameter Configuration Parameters>Optimization>Conditional input branch execution  is checked. Checking this option potentially improves execution speed when the model contains Switch and Multiport Switch blocks. An Assignment block is inside a For Iterator subsystem or a While iterator subsystem This Assignment block is connected to the input port of a Switch or Multiport Switch block inside the same iterator subsystem.
Workaround: Download and unzip the attachment to a folder and add the folder to the MATLAB path using the addpath() command. Run the diagnostic utility on your model using the following syntax:ret = DetectForBugReport1020454('modelname')For a model that is unaffected by this issue, the utility returns an empty array. If this issue impacts your model, the utility returns an array of structures with the following fields:AssignmentBlock : The Assignment block which is inside a For/While-Iterator subsystem and on a conditional execution branchCECOwnerBlock : Block which creates the conditional execution branch mentioned aboveCECOwnerPortIndex : Port index of the CECOwner block, for which the conditional execution branch mentioned above is createdIteratorSubsystem : For/While-Iterator subsystem which contains the Assignment blockTo work around the issue, you can either uncheck the parameter Configuration Parameters>Optimization>Conditional input branch execution, or you can configure the output of each of the affected Assignment blocks in your model as a test point. To do the latter, right click on the signal line, select Properties, check the box Test point in the Logging and accessibility tab of the dialog box that appears, then click OK.Change log:October 8, 2014: attachment_1020454_6a_through_13b_20141015.zip: updated, utility no longer closes the model after it runs.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: imfill, imreconstruct, imregionalmax and padarray functions error during code generation for 3-D, variable-sized inputs
Description: Generating code for imfill , imregionalmax , and imreconstruct  fails with 3-D, variable-sized inputs. >> codegen imfill -args {coder.typeof(1, [2 2 2], 1)} ??? All inputs must be constant. Error in ==> conndef Line: 8 Column: 1 Generating code for padarray  with 3-D, variable-sized inputs succeeds at compile-time but fails at runtime when you provide empty inputs. To illustrate, the following example creates a small function that uses padarray .  function paddedIm = test1(im) paddedIm = padarray(im,[3 3 1],Inf,'pre'); end When you generate code for this function, it succeeds, as shown in the following. >> codegen test1 -args {coder.typeof(0, [45 45 45], 1)} When you run the function, passing an empty array, it fails. >> test1_mex([]) Sizes mismatch: [1][2] ~= [1][3]. Error in padarray (line 17) sizeB = varSizeA + padSize; Error in test1 (line 2) paddedIm = padarray(im,[3 3],Inf,'pre');

Summary: MATLAB Production Server may exit unexpectedly when the system is under heavy load.
Description: This issue is limited to configurations in which --num-threads is set to a value greater than 1. However, it is recommended that this patch be installed on all R2013b+ installations. This is due to a race condition introduced in the R2013b+ release. This issue does not effect any other release.
Workaround: Download the attached .zip file and extract the contents to the R2013b+ MATLAB Production Server installation folder.

Summary: Using inputParser with ambiguous parameter names and true values for KeepUnmatched and PartialMatching properties crashes MATLAB
Description: If an inputParser  object has parameter names that share a leading substring and its PartialMatching  and KeepUnmatched  properties are both true , a call to the parse  method with an ambiguous leading substring crashes MATLAB.  By default, the inputParser  constructor sets the KeepUnmatched  property to false .   For example,    p = inputParser;   addParameter(p,'thing1',1);   addParameter(p,'thing2',2);   p.KeepUnmatched = true;   parse(p,'thing',3); crashes MATLAB.
Workaround: To avoid a MATLAB crash, set the PartialMatching property to false.  This disables all partial matching of parameter names.  For example,  p = inputParser;   addParameter(p,'thing1',1);  addParameter(p,'thing2',2);  p.KeepUnmatched = true;  p.PartialMatching = false;  parse(p,'thing',3);  p.Unmatched  ans =      thing: 3

Summary: Help Browser problems when IE 11 is installed on 64-bit Windows
Description: The following problems occur in the Help browser on 64-bit Windows? if Internet Explorer? 11 is installed:Copying text using Ctrl+C  does not work  Ctrl+F  causes MATLAB to become unresponsive The context menu does not appear when right-clicking Links in the Contents pane of the Help Browser may not expand

Summary: "Failed to get RMI data ..." warning while running Simulink Project Dependency Analysis
Description: In Simulink Project, if you run Dependency Analysis with Options  > Find requirements documents  enabled, warnings about Requirements Management Interface (RMI) data, similar to the following, might appear: Failed to get RMI data for sm_lib/Frames and  Transforms/World Frame. Library "sm_lib" is not loaded.  Failed to get RMI data for nesl_utility/PS-Simulink  Converter. Library "nesl_utility" is not loaded.
Workaround: If a warning refers to a built-in library, that is, a library installed with MATLAB or Simulink, disregard the warning.If a warning refers to a custom library, that is, a library that is not installed with MATLAB or Simulink, load this library and then run Dependency Analysis.

Summary: Data dependency violation in Simulink.findVars but not in normal update diagram
Description: If a submodel in your model reference hierarchy uses a function-call or action subsystem, compilation of the top model may fail when you run Simulink.findVars  on the top model. However, a regular update diagram operation does not return errors. The compilation failure that occurs when you use Simulink.findVars  is caused by a data dependency violation arising from the use of a function-call or action subsystem.
Workaround: Use the cached search method when you run Simulink.findVars in such models.If you are programmatically searching for variables, make the following changes to Simulink.findVars calls.For update diagram: set_param(model, 'SimulationCommand', 'update')To call Simulink.findVars using cached search method: vars = Simulink.findVars(model, 'SearchMethod', 'cached')

Summary: If a Stateflow Simulink Function has the same name as a compatible math function, the Stateflow compatibility checks incorrectly warn and code inspection exits with a segmentation violation
Description: If a Stateflow Simulink Function has the same name as a compatible math function, the Stateflow compatibility checks incorrectly warn and code inspection exits with a segmentation violation. If a Simulink function has the same name as a math function, Simulink Code Inspector incorrectly processes the Simulink function as a math function. As a result, Simulink Code Inspector runs one or both of the following compatibility checks applicable to math functions on Simulink functions: Check usage of Stateflow states > Check that no actions contain a function whose argment is of an invalid data type  Check usage of Stateflow transitions > Check that no actions contain a function whose argment is of an invalid data type   The compability checks incorrectly warn that Simulink Code Inspector does not support actions that contain a function whose argument is of an invalid data type . For example, consider Simulink function y = sin(u,v)  for int32  inputs u  and v  that redefines the sine function by computing the sine of u  and adding v  to it.  The compatibility checks incorrectly warn that Simulink Code Inspector does not support actions that contain a function whose argument is of an invalid data type .  Code inspection of a Stateflow chart that contains a Simulink function with the same name as compatible math function exits with a segmentation violation.

Summary: Shift Arithmetic block does not generate correct shift value within a masked reusable subsystem for fixed point inputs.
Description: The Shift Arithmetic block does not generate correct shift value during code generation, if all the following conditions are true: Shift Arithmetic block is in a masked reusable subsystem.  There are several such masked reusable subsystems in the model. The Shift Arithmetic block parameter Bits to shift  is used as a mask parameter. Input to the Shift Arithmetic block is a fixed point data type. The Shift Arithmetic block parameter Bits to shift  Source is set to Dialog . The checkbox Configuration Parameters->Optimization->Signals and Parameters->Inline parameters  is not checked.
Workaround: In the reusable subsystem block, change the subsystem parameter Function Packaging to Nonreusable function. This causes individual correct functions to be generated.

Summary: HDL code generation fails for bus input to Stateflow Chart or MATLAB Function block
Description: When a bus signal is connected to the input port of a Stateflow Chart or MATLAB Function block, and the input port data type is set to Bus, HDL code generation may fail. In the Model Explorer, the input port DataType for the bus shows as:Bus: (bus obj) where (bus obj) is the name of a user-defined bus object in the Base Workspace.
Workaround: Set the DataType of the bus input to "Inherit: Same as Simulink" instead of "Bus: (bus obj)".

Summary: Compiled application using MCC may fail when using Image Acquisition Toolbox
Description: In R2013b, an application compiled using MATLAB Compiler may fail to run successfully if you are using functions from Image Acquisition Toolbox. The application builds fine, however fails to find 2 libraries when the application is run against the MCR.
Workaround: The workaround requires installation of an updated MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) with the patch that fixes deployment of Image Acquisition Toolbox functions.  Please contact MathWorks Technical Support for the workaround.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing imaginary part of result from binary operation
Description: In C and C++ code generated from MATLAB?  code, a binary operation between complex and noncomplex inputs can produce an incorrect answer. If one of the inputs is noncomplex with a fixed size and the other input is complex with a variable size, the result of a binary operation can be missing the imaginary part.
Workaround: Detect the binary operation that erroneously produces noncomplex output. Create a complex input from the noncomplex input. For example, suppose that a is complex and b is noncomplex. If a.*b produces noncomplex output, replace it with a .* complex(b,0).

Summary: Remote verification fails on HPC cluster
Description: A remote execution on an HPC server fails at the very beginning of the verification.  Specifically, it fails to unpack the archive from the client, giving the message: Checking license ... License is OK Remark: Executing Polyspace Code Prover as a batch job on a MATLAB worker (in <working_folder>) Extracting Polyspace data in <working_folder> ...Extraction of Polyspace data completed Unable to load client archive in <working_folder>
Workaround: Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. Summary: Constellation Diagram block displays an error even if the MeasurementInterval property is less than the SymbolsToDisplay property in some cases.
Description: Enabling the signal measurement for the Constellation Diagram block can result in a property mismatch error if the MeasurementInterval property is greater than 256 (the default SymbolsToDisplay property used). ?
Workaround: Update the <matlabroot>/toolbox/comm/commutilities/+comm/+scopes/SignalQualityDialog.m file in the installation by replacing the following check in the checkMeasurementInterval subfunctionvalue>dlg.SymbolsToDisplaytovalue>str2double(dlg.Measurer.SymbolsToDisplay)?

Summary: The Target Graphics Display prints repeatedly the following message "Scope #, does not exist".
Description: This is a bug with the xPC Target Explorer. Everything works as expected when the Explorer is opened and connected to the target onto which is already loaded a target application that has scopes. However, if you then build and download a target application to the target that has no scopes or has fewer scopes, the kernel prints the message "Scope #, does not exist" repeatedly on the target display.
Workaround: To apply the fix make sure MATLAB is completely closed.?Replace the orignal "Desktop.Xpc.ScopeExplorer.dll" from the following ML installion folder:<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\xpcmngr\binwith the proper file from the attachment.?????

Summary: Initial compilation of model fails due to Simulink.Parameter object resolution error
Description: The initial compilation of a model can fail if you pass a Simulink.Parameter  object from the base workspace as a mask parameter value through multiple mask layers. The compilation failure is accompanied by an error message similar to the following one. Error evaluating properties of Simulink.Parameter object 'P_base' in workspace 'base' (used by block 'path/to/a/block')  Caused by:Model 'MDL' is using workspace variables 'P_base' and 'P_mask' but these variables refer to the same object. Update the MATLAB code that created these objects to create separate objects. Use the copy method to copy Simulink data objects  You can resolve the error by recompiling the model.

Summary: Functions in the C code generated by the SystemVerilog DPI component have different arguments than those same functions in the SystemVerilog wrapper.
Description: If the Simulink subsystem for code generation contains continuous states, then functions in the C code generated by the SystemVerilog DPI component generator have different arguments than those same functions in the SystemVerilog wrapper. For example, the generated C code for the declaration of the output function may look like this: DPI_DLLESPEC void DPI_VCO_PN_output(); However, that same declaration looks like this in the SystemVerilog wrapper: import "DPI-C" function void DPI_VCO_PN_output(output real VCO_PN_Y_VCO); The generated C function takes no argument, while the SystemVerilog function takes one argument. This change to the function arguments causes a run-time error during simulation in the HDL simulator. ? ? ? ?
Workaround:
Installation instructions

  1. Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.
  2. Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.
  3. Exit MATLAB.
  4. Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.
  5. Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file:
    • Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.
    • Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):
      unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>
  6. Extract the files from the .zip file.
    • Windows Systems*:
      1. Open the .zip file.
      2. Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.
    • Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):
      cd <MATLAB>
      unzip <.zip file>
  7. Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

*Notes for Windows Systems:

  1. You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.
  2. If you see "Error: Access is denied" when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.
  3. From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.
  4. Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: dxpcmdsdemo stops with an error message in R2013b release.
Description: Dxpcmdsdemo shows error message "Measurement of function execution times is not supported for xPC Target" when invoked from the Matlab command window.
Workaround: In the Matlab command window, type the following commandsopen([matlabroot '\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpcdemos\dxpcmds6t'])set_param(bdroot,'CodeProfilingInstrumentation','off');save_system(bdroot);

Summary: Code inspection fails when the output of a conditional subsystem is input to a Vector Concatenate block
Description: Code inspection can fail for a model when the output of a conditionally executed subsystem is input to a Vector Concatenate block. Conditionally executed subsystems include Triggered and Enabled Subsystems.
Workaround: Consider inserting a Signal Conversion block between the conditional subsystem and the Vector Concatenate block. In the Single Conversion block, on the block parameters pane, select Exclude the block from 'Block reduction' optimization.?

Summary: Incorrect output from gradientm function for input crossing longitude 180
Description: The fourth output of the gradientm  function, which is the local east component of the gradient, can be off by a large amount if the input grid crosses the 180-degree meridian. In the case of a regular data grid, two columns in this output argument (at or very close to longitude 180) typically contain incorrect values. The output values at other longitudes are unaffected.
Workaround: The outputs of gradientm should be unchanged by a constant shift in longitude. In the case of a regular data grid, this fact enables the following workaround: add or subtract a constant from the latitude limits before calling gradientm, shifting it to eliminate any crossings of the 180-degree meridian. In the case of most non-polar geolocated data grids, a similar workaround can be used: add or subtract a constant from the longitude mesh. Post-processing is not required in either case.

Summary: If generated code contains calls to functions that are not C standard library functions, code inspection can fail
Description: If generated code contains calls to functions that are not C standard library functions, code inspection can fail. The failure is due to a type mismatch in the function call return. If the model contains Lookup Table blocks, Model Reference blocks and user-defined code replacement libriaries, the generated code might contain a call to a function that is not a C standard library function, resulting in code inspection failure.

Description: Simulink Design Verifier stops analysis, and reports an error that looks like the following: Internal Error parsing CGEL file at line 14, char 99 This occurs if you run Simulink Design Verifier on a model where all of the following are true: The model's Inport blocks output signal buses defined by a bus object. The bus object contains an element that is also a bus.? The nested bus object contains elements that the model does not use.

Description: If a MEX function created by MATLAB Coder?  returns a dynamic structure matrix of zero size, the structure has no fields. The original MATLAB program returns an empty structure with fields that have no value.

Summary: Preprocessor conditionals are not used when defining mpt.Parameter objects in the generated code
Description: When generating code for subsystem variants using preprocessor conditionals, the code generator attempts to conditionally define parameters used solely by one variant. However, if the parameter is defined using an mpt.Parameter  object and the object specifies one of the following storage classes:  Const  ConstVolatile  Define  ExportToFile  Global  ImportedDefine  Volatile   the parameter's data is unconditionally defined in the generated code.
Workaround: Instead of using an mpt.Parameter object with a custom storage class, use a Simulink.Parameter object with the same custom storage class.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer for variable-size arrays when left-hand side and right-hand side have different matrix shapes
Description: Consider the following example: function buf = example(buf, param)assert(isa(buf, 'double')); assert(all(size(buf) == [1 4 4])); assert(isa(param, 'double')); assert(param <= 3); q = param; q1 = 3; buf(1,1:1+q,1:1+q) = squeeze(buf(1,1:1+q1,1:1+q1)); In the last assignment, the left-hand side has shape 1 x :4 x :4 and the right-hand side has shape :4 x :4. For variable-size arrays, the shape mismatch on singleton dimensions causes the generation of  incorrect code.
Workaround: If there's a dimension (including singleton dimensions) mismatch, write the matrix/array assignment as a loop:tmp = squeeze(buf(1,1:1+q1,1:1+q1));for i = 1:q   for j = 1:q      buf(1,i,j) = tmp(i,j);    endend

Summary: Substring '=[' in  MATLAB? source might cause compilation report failure
Description: If a MATLAB?  source program has strings containing multiple occurrences of the substring '=[', then generation of the compilation report might fail. The error message is "Colon operands must be real scalars."
Workaround: Recode the source string so that the characters '=' and '[' are not contiguous in a given string. For example, instead of[ '=[ foo =[' ]use[ '=' '[ foo =' '[' ]Both of these syntaxes represent the same string. This recoding does not cause a semantic difference.

Summary: If a Stateflow chart contains Simulink functions and explicit function-call events, code inspection may exit with a segmentation violation
Description: If a Stateflow chart contains Simulink functions and explicit function-call  events, code inspection may exit with a segmentation violation. Simulink Code Inspector can successfully verify charts with either explicit events or Simulink functions, but it cannot verify charts with both explicit events and Simulink functions. If a chart contains both an explicit event and a Simulink function, during code inspection, the explicit event port number incorrectly resolves to the Simulink function port, resulting in a segmentation violation.
Workaround: Simulink Code Inspector can verify charts with either explicit events or Simulink functions, but not both. Consider either of the following:Represent explicit events as Simulink functions, so that only Simulink functions are used in the chart.Represent Simulink functions as explicit events, so that only explicit events are used in the chart.

Summary: Weld joints in kinematic loops cause spurious kinematic-constraint errors
Description: If a model contains a weld joint in a kinematic loop, simulation can fail with the error message: 'JointBlockName' kinematic constraints cannot be maintained.   A kinematic loop is a closed circuit of rigid bodies and joints. The four-bar mechanism is an example of a kinematic loop. Your model has a kinematic loop if it contains: A closed circuit of SimMechanics blocks representing rigid bodies and joints. An open sequence of SimMechanics blocks, also representing rigid bodies and joints, that begins and ends with rigid connections to the world frame.  This error typically occurs when a mechanism reaches a singular configuration, causing the mechanism to lose one or more degrees of freedom. However, when a kinematic loop contains a weld joint, the same error can occur even in ordinary configurations for which kinematic constraints can be maintained.
Workaround: If a Weld Joint block is not essential, consider removing it from the model. You can replace a Weld Joint block with:Rigid connection lineRigid Transform block using the default parametrization (no rotation, no translation)Some Weld Joint blocks are essential in a model. SimMechanics requires each kinematic loop to have at least one joint block without motion inputs or computed actuation forces/torques. Weld Joint blocks enable you to satisfy this condition when all other joint blocks must have motion inputs or computed actuation forces/torques.To fix the simulation error in such models, you can use an alternative approach:Replace the Weld joint block with a 1-DOF Joint blockˇŞi.e., Prismatic Joint or Revolute Joint.Set the joint spring and damping coefficients to large values. Large spring and damping coefficients ensure that the joint remains relatively motionless during simulation. (Note, however, that excessively large values can slow down simulation.)

Summary: Constellation Diagram results in an error for a column vector reference constellation when Signal Quality panel is turned on.
Description: When the ReferenceConstellation  property of a comm.ConstellationDiagram System object or the Reference constellation  parameter of a Constellation Diagram block is a column vector, it results in an error when you choose to display the Signal Quality  panel. To reproduce the error, type the following in the MATLAB Command Window: hMod = comm.QPSKModulator;hDiagram = comm.ConstellationDiagram('ReferenceConstellation', hMod.constellation);step(hDiagram, step(hMod, randi([0 3], 10, 1)));  When the constellation diagram shows up, click its "Signal Quality" button and observe the error: Error using comm.EVM/set.AverageConstellationPower (line 170)Expected AverageConstellationPower to be a scalar.
Workaround: In the MATLAB Command Window, type the following:cd(matlabroot)edit toolbox/comm/commutilities/+comm/+scopes/SignalQuality.mChange the line 81 of SignalQuality.m mag = abs(value); to mag = abs(str2num(value));

Summary: Existing file deleted during code generation with the To Multimedia Block or the vision.VideoFileWriter System object.
Description: During code generation, the To Multimedia  Block and the vision.VideoFileWriter  System object deletes an existing video file. The error occurs when the output file name matches an existing video file name.
Workaround: Move your existing video file to a different directory or rename the file before generating code.

Summary: Scope displays zeros for filter code generated from model configured for external mode and ARM Cortex-M target hardware and code replacement
Description: When all the following conditions exist, the scope displaying output for a filter block shows all zeros: Model includes a filter block Filter block is directly connected and sends its output to a scope block Target Hardware is configured as ARM Cortex-M3 (QEMU) or STM32F4-Discovery Code replacement library is configured as ARM Cortex-M Model configured for External mode for host/target communication
Workaround: To workaround this issue, for each filter block in the model that is connected directly to a scope block, insert a Gain block that has a unity gain between the filter block and scope.?

Summary: Stateflow transition labels move up on every text edit
Description: Each time you commit a change to a Stateflow transition label, Stateflow incorrectly adds a small offset is to the label's vertical position. You commit a modification by clicking away from the label.

Summary: Mode declaration group definition errors reported after importing AUTOSAR ModeDeclarationGroup from arxml into a model
Description: If you import arxml  that contains a mode-switch interface, with a mode-receiver port and a mode declaration group definition, the arxml  importer creates a dynamic enumerated type for the imported ModeDeclarationGroup . However, dynamic enumeration is not serialized to disk, so you cannot save the Simulink enumerated type in a MATLAB file. If you save the model and subsequently open it in a new MATLAB session, the enumerated type for the ModeDeclarationGroup  is missing. For example, when the model is recompiled for simulation, the software reports mode declaration group definition errors.

Summary: Pointer to a tuneable parameter of the wrong data type might be passed to a function in generated code
Description: A pointer to a tuneable parameter of the wrong data type might be passed to a function in the generated code.  In this case, the compiler reports a warning similar to the following: model.c: In function 'model_output':model.c:112: warning: passing argument 2 of 'plook_u32u32u32n31_linc_f' from incompatible pointer typemodel.c:32: note: expected 'const uint32_T *' but argument is of type 'int16_T *' Previously, an error was reported during code generation and code would not be produced.
Workaround: Set the data type of the tunable parameter to match the expected data type for the function.

Summary: Application Compiler failure on Windows XP
Description: Use Application Compiler on a Windows XP machine to package a standalone application. Open output folder and launch for_redistribution\MyAppInstaller_web.exe . 3 error messages pop up one after the other: 1. The specified resource type cannot be found in the image file.2. Failed to get the payload. 3. Could not source the install agent The executable fails to install anything.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Rate Transition blocks in series can cause incorrect activation of Enabled Subsystems.
Description: If two Rate Transition blocks are connected in series between a continuous or periodic input sample time and an asynchronous output sample time, this modeling pattern can produce incorrect simulation results. For example, in the model bug.slx attached here, the ˇ®NoOpRTBˇŻ block is configured to have minimum delay and maximum memory efficiency. The output port of the block feeds signals into a Function-Call subsystem asynchronously executed through the input port In2. This signal drives an Enabled subsystem contained inside the Function-Call subsystem. Simulink executes the Function-Call subsystem asynchronously according to the interrupt A1. However, in the figure NoOp.png attached here, ˇ®ScopeD1ˇŻ shows the Enabled subsystem activated at a sample time D3 instead of A1. This represents a bug. When you use the 'Ensure data integrity during data transfer' option on the ˇ®NoOpRTBˇŻ block, the Enabled subsystem gets correctly enabled at the asyncronous sample rate A1 (see Db_buf.png attached). When you don't use this option, the Enabled subsystem is incorrectly enabled at periodic rate D3.
Workaround: 1. Avoid two Rate Transition blocks connected in series, or,2. When the output of a Rate Transition block drives an asynchronous Function-Call subsystem, select the 'Ensure data integrity during data transfer' option.

Summary: A model containing enumerated data types and a MATLAB Function block may fail to build

You do not use the enumerated data types anywhere else in the model including as an input or output type to the MATLAB Function block.  ?

For limitations, check the S-parameter documentation and "Compare Time and Frequency Domain Simulation Options for S-parameters" demo.Option 2. Use 4-port s-parameter model. This option requires modifying s-parameter file or data. The "fake" 4th port needs to be grounded with the s-parameter reference impedance (typically 50 Ohm resistor).Option 3. Summary: Traceability from model Web view of code generation report to the code can break
Description: In the model Web view of the code generation report, if there are block names in the model that include double quotes, blocks might not trace to the code. However, model-to-code traceability is not broken.
Workaround: Remove double quotes from block names then regenerate code and the code generation report.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer when coder.const input expression passes structure array constants to an extrinsic function?
Description: If structure array constants are created  by the code generation software and coder.const  passes them to an extrinsic function, coder.const  produces an incorrect answer. For example, suppose that the file example.m  contains example  and myconst.   function y = examplecoder.extrinsic('matlab_function');y = coder.const(matlab_function(myconst()));function C = myconstC_scalar.f1 = int32(92);C_scalar.f2 = int32(42);C_scalar.f3 = int32(4711);C_scalar.f4 = int32(123);C= repmat(C_scalar,[1,2]); Suppose that in the same folder, the file matlab_function.m   contains matlab_function . function y = matlab_function(u)y = u(1).f2; Compile example  and run the MEX function. >> codegen example.m>> example_mex     92 The answer 92 is incorrect. The correct answer is 42. ?
Workaround: Do not pass structure array constants created by the code generation software to an extrinsic function that is input to coder.const. Instead, use another extrinsic function to create the structure array constants in the MATLAB environment.To correct the previous example:1) Put the MATLAB function myconst in the file myconst.m.function C = myconstC_scalar.f1 = int32(92);C_scalar.f2 = int32(42);C_scalar.f3 = int32(4711);C_scalar.f4 = int32(123);C= repmat(C_scalar,[1,2]);2) Declare myconst as an extrinsic function.coder.extrinsic('matlab_function');coder.extrinsic('myconst');y = coder.const(matlab_function(myconst()));The answer is correct because the structure arrays are created in the MATLAB environment.

Summary: Combining Data Store Read Block elements into a bus can slow down performance
Description: The edit and compile time performance of the model may be adversely impacted if a Data Store Read block has multiple output ports and the signals from these output ports are combined into a bus using a Bus Creator block.
Workaround: Output the entire bus from the Data Store Read block such that the block has only one output port, and use a Bus Selector to select the same set of signals as originally specified in the "Element Selection" tab of the Data Store Read block.

Summary: containers.Map constructor converts scalar numeric values to logical values when its key is a single string
Description: If a key is a single string, and its value is a scalar numeric data type, the containers.Map  constructor converts the value to a logical  data type.  For example, if you create a map using     m = containers.Map('aKey',4); the resulting map has ValueType = 'logical'  instead of double  and the value of the key is true  (logical 1 ) instead of 4 . This conversion does not occur when the keys and values are non-scalar or the key type is non-string.
Workaround: This conversion is avoided using one of the following workarounds.Use cell arrays to define keys, and/or values.  For example,m = containers.Map({'aKey'},{4});Set the parameter 'UniformValues' to true. For example,m = containers.Map('aKey',4,'UniformValues',true);Setting 'UniformValues' to true in the constructor indicates that all values will be the same data type.  This prevents the constructor from converting the scalar values to logical, but also prevents values of different type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reused function-caller subsystems may generate incorrect code
Description: In some cases, function-caller subsystems that are reused may generate incorrect code when: The two instances contain the same algorithm The outputs or merge blocks are connected to different signals The "Pass reusable subsystem outputs as" parameter is set to "Individual Arguments"  Function-caller subsystems are systems that generate function calls, like Stateflow charts, and S-functions.  For clarification, download, and see the attached model. The attached model demonstrates the behavior in versions R2010a and later, however, the same pattern may produce the result in versions R2009a and later.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, the "Pass reusable subsystem outputs as" parameter can be changed to "Structure reference."

Summary: When the variable 'i' is defined in the base workspace, incorrect results are returned by the 'Squaring Timing Recovery' block.
Description: The 'Squaring Timing Recovery' block in the commtimrec2 library may produce incorrect results if a variable "i" is defined in the base workspace. ?
Workaround: Clear the variable "i" in the base workspace or redefine it to be sqrt(-1), prior to simulating a model that uses the Squaring Timing Recovery block.

Summary: Simulation inside parfor in Rapid Accelerator mode with up-to-date check turned off throws an error when you do not save your model after adding a to/fromfile.
Description: Simulation inside parfor in Rapid Accelerator mode with up-to-date check turned off throws an error when all of these conditions are valid:You have added a  tofile or fromfile block to the model. You do not save the model after making changes. You build the model to simulate in Rapid Accelerator mode using Simulink.BlockDiagram.buildRapidAcceleratorTarget(). You run a simulation inside parfor using Rapid Accelerator mode with up-to-date check turned off.  When ALL of these conditions are true, Simulink throws an error in the 13b version. This is a departure from the behavior of this mode in older versions. This applies only to cases where you add a tofile or fromfile block to the model.
Workaround: Save your model after making changes. It is the saved copy of your model that is distributed to parallel workers when simulating in parfor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: External reset for Discrete Filter block does not work in code generation for DF1 and DF1T structures
Description: If an external reset is selected and a reset event occurs, the Discrete Filter block does not reset its state as expected in code generation mode, where the filter structure is set to either Direct Form I or Direct Form I transposed.
Workaround: Set the filter structure to Direct Form II or Direct Form II transposed when an external reset is selected.

Description: Three required libraries that support the Computer Vision System Toolbox do not get installed. The absence of these libraries cause System objects and a subset of functions to return errors similar to the ones shown below. Errors seen in Mac OS X or Linux: Error using compSetParameters:  Unable to load /Applications/MATLAB_R2013b.app/toolbox/vision/visionutilities/visionmex/mvipcolorconv.mexmaci64 [error: dlopen(/Applications/MATLAB_R2013b.app/toolbox/vision/visionutilities/visionmex/mvipcolorconv.mexmaci64, 6): Library not loaded: @rpath/libmwSimulinkBlock.dylib Referenced from: /Applications/MATLAB_R2013b.app/toolbox/vision/visionutilities/visionmex/mvipcolorconv.mexmaci64 Reason: image not found]  Error seen in Windows: MATLAB:dispatcher:loadLibrary Can't load 'c:\program files\matlab\r2013b\bin\win32\systemobject_sfunmcos.dll': The specified module could not be found.   Caught MathWorks::System::FatalException   Note:If your installation includes either the Simulink, DSP System Toolbox, or the Image Acquisition Toolbox products, you will not experience this error. These products trigger the installation of the required libraries.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Summary: Multiport Switch block using floating point type control input can produce incorrect derived range analysis results
Description: In some cases, using a floating point signal for the control input of the Multiport Switch block can produce incorrect derived range analysis results.  The following conditions must be true to trigger this bug: The control input is a floating point type (single, double or scaled double) Either the initial condition or all possible values of the control input signal are outside the range of a 32 bit signed integer type  The range of a 32 bit signed integer value is -2^31 to 2^31-1. A control signal with data type double and an initial condition value of 2^32 triggers this bug. Triggering this bug usually results missing range results for model entities where they are expected. In some cases the calculated ranges could be imprecise or too small.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink? Coder? might generate unnecessary UNUSED_PARAMETER macro
Description: Simulink Coder might generate an unnecessary UNUSED_PARAMETER  macro for a task ID(tid) variable that is not declared in an empty root level entry function. The generated code might fail to compile if the UNUSED_PARAMETER  macro contains any code.
Workaround: Place a block that requires time, such as the Digital Clock block, with a rate of the empty rate grouped function into the model.

Summary: Model does not rebuild in accelerator mode when referenced enumeration changes in value
Description: If your model references an enumeration workspace variable, changing the variable does not trigger model rebuild during simulation in accelerator mode. Consequently, you may see incorrect results during simulation.
Workaround: After building your model, if you change the value of an enumeration workspace variable referenced by your model, delete the corresponding slprj folder that was generated for that model during the previous build.For example, if your model myAccelModel.slx references an enumeration workspace variable that you changed after building the model, delete the folder slprj\sim\myAccelModel. The deletion will cause the model to rebuild when you simulate in accelerator mode.

Summary: Temporal logic conditions can evaluate to true before the specified time near the start of simulation
Description: Near the start of simulation, absolute-time temporal logic can evaluate to true before the specified time is reached. For example, a chart with a transition after(2,sec)  with a fixed-time step solver of 1.0 second can evaluate to true at time = 1.0 sec instead of time = 2.0 sec. This bug occurs under the following conditions: You use a Moore machine or a Stateflow chart with the chart property "Execute (enter) Chart at Initialization". You use an absolute-time temporal logic statement, such as after(2, sec) , in the initial state or in a transition that has the initial state as the source.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report according to your system architecture and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see "Error: Access is denied" when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Ordinal arrays that were created and saved to a mat file in R2013a or earlier do not load correctly in R2013b
Description: If you create an ordinal array in R2013a or earlier and save it to a mat file, and then load that mat file in R2013b, the array does not load properly as an ordinal array. For example, if you have such an array x , and if you use the less than relational operator, the operator does not recognize x  as an ordinal  array, and you see this error: >> x < 'A'Error using < (line 25)Relational comparisons are not allowed for categorical arrays that are not ordinal.  ?
Workaround: Load the mat file, then call ordinal on the array. For example:>> load myOrdinalData.mat>> whos  Name        Size            Bytes  Class      Attributes  x         100x1               648  ordinal              >> x = ordinal(x);If you wish, you can now save the array x to a new mat file, which you can load normally using R2013b. However, you will not be able to load the new mat file in R2013a or earlier.Alternatively, modify lines 402-403 of the file MATLABROOT\toolbox\shared\statslib\@ordinal\ordinal.m to read b.isProtected = true; b.isOrdinal = true;where MATLABROOT is the root directory of your MATLAB installation.

Summary: VideoReader fails when reading some files on Linux? with Gstreamer? version older than 0.10.22
Description: On Linux? distributions with a Gstreamer? version older than 0.10.22, VideoReader  fails when attempting to read some video formats supported by Gstreamer, such as MPEG-1 and H.264. The creation of the VideoReader  object fails. For example, this code:vidObj = VideoReader('xylophone.mpg');  fails with the following error:Error using VideoReader/init (line 450)The VideoReader plugin libmwgstreamerplugin failed to load properly.Error in VideoReader (line 147)            obj.init(fileName);

Summary: Models created in R2012b or earlier with self activity enabled switch to child activity output when opened in R2013a or R2013b
Description: Prior to R2013a, active state output was limited to the ability of statesto output their self activity, a Boolean output indicating whether or not a particular state is active. In R2013a, child activity output was introduced, allowing a chart or state to output a variable indicating which of its children is active. Models created before R2013a with self activity enabled switch to child activity output when opened in R2013a or R2013b. This unexpected change in output mode can lead to answer mismatches between R2013a or R2013b and previous releases because the model configuration has changed without the user intending to do so.
