Summary: Simulation and code generation give wrong answers for Modulus operation in Math Function block when both inputs have signed integer data type with different signs, and divide exactly.
Description: In the Math Function Block, choose mod  function. The output will be wrong for both simulation and code generation when the inputs meet the following conditions Both inputs have signed interger data types The two inputs have different signs The first input is an integral multiple of the second input  The expected output should be 0, however, the actual output is the same as the second input value. Both simulation and code generation will give this incorrect answer.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use the MATLAB Function block and use the modulus function inside that, as shown in the sample implementation belowfunction y = fcn(u1, u2)y = mod(u1,u2);end

Summary: Code generation fails with internal error: Database has no function 'rt_zcFcn'?
Description: Code generation for a model fails with an internal error if all of the following conditions are true: The model requires the zero-crossing detection function rt_zcFcn . For example, the model contains a trigger subsystem. The data type of the trigger signal is not available on the hardware[1] . The Number of bits: int  value for the hardware[1]  is less than 32, and the bit size of the trigger signal data type is not larger than the Number of bits: int  value for the hardware[1] . For example, Number of bits: int  equals 16, and the data type of the trigger signal is int8 .  Under these conditions, the software displays an error that includes the following text: Database has no function 'rt_zcFcn' [1]  In this description, "hardware" refers to the production hardware if the model configuration parameter Test hardware is the same as production hardware  (ProdEqTarget ) is set to on . Otherwise, "hardware" refers to the test hardware.
Workaround: To work around this bug, set the data type of the trigger signal to int32.

Summary: Cannot find product when sending verification job to the server
Description: If Polyspace 8.5 (R2013a) and Polyspace 9.0 (R2013b) are both installed, the error message The Polyspace 8.5 (R2013a) product was not found  may appear when you send a R2013a job to the Polyspace server. Once the Polyspace service is upgraded to the R2013b version, the service no longer recognizes that the Polyspace R2013a kernel is installed.
Workaround: Installation instructions on cluster side (R2013b)Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your R2013b Polyspace installation. By default this is in C:\Program Files\MATLAB or in /usr/local/MATLAB on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB_ROOT> in subsequent steps.Stop mdce and polyspaced services using <MATLAB_ROOT>\polyspace\bin\polsypace-rl-manager.exeDownload the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB_ROOT> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB_ROOT>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB_ROOT> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <MATLAB_ROOT>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate 'read & execute' permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB_ROOT> folder.Restart mdce and polyspaced services.

Summary: fopen fails for relative path from root drive on Windows
Description: The fopen  function fails and returns -1  when creating a new file or opening an existing file. This problem occurs on Windows? systems when the file path does not include the drive letter. On Windows systems, specifying a path with a leading '\'  is an alias for a path relative to the root drive of the current directory. For example, the following command should create or open mytest.txt  when called in any subdirectory of the C:  directory:>> fid = fopen('\Program Files\mytest.txt','w') However, fopen  fails to resolve the root directory alias and returns -1 .
Workaround: Include the root drive in the file name input to fopen:fid = fopen('C:\Program Files\mytest.txt','w')

Summary: The code generator can fail to generate model reference code for models that have custom storage classes with file scope
Description: If both of these conditions apply, the code generator fails to generate model reference code: Model has Storage class  set to FileScope (Custom) . Simulink signal object is attached to a signal or state in the root level of the model.  The product displays the following error message: Unable to find StandaloneInitializeFcn within the System scope
Workaround: Place root level blocks into a new root level Atomic Subsystem block.In the Atomic Subsystem block parameters dialog, on the Code Generation pane:Set Function packaging to Nonreusable function.Do not set File name option to Auto.

Summary: Using mxGPUCopyFromMxArray on an mxArray that contains GPU data prevents the GPU memory from being freed properly.
Description: When writing a MEX-function for use with GPU data, using the function mxGPUCopyFromMxArray  on an mxArray that contains GPU data prevents GPU memory from being freed properly. The associated GPU memory is "leaked" and is not available again until you reset the GPU device.
Workaround: The function mxGPUCopyFromMxArray is a convenience function that creates a writeable copy of the array on the GPU device from either a host or device input. If you do not need a writeable copy, use mxGPUCreateFromMxArray instead.If you need a writable copy of the mxArray, you can explicitly check whether the input is on the host or device, and perform the copying yourself. If the input is on the device, use mxGPUCopyGPUArray to create the copy. If the input is on the host, use mxGPUCopyFromMxArray. The following code snippet is a complete replacement for mxGPUCopyFromMxArray that you can use in your code:mxGPUArray * mxGPUCopyFromMxArray2(mxArray const * mx) {    if (mxIsGPUArray(mx)) {        mxGPUArray const * tmp = mxGPUCreateFromMxArray(mx);        mxGPUArray * returnValue = mxGPUCopyGPUArray(tmp);        mxGPUDestroyGPUArray(tmp);        return returnValue;    } else {        return mxGPUCopyFromMxArray(mx));    }}???

Summary: Polyspace for Model Link option AdditionalFileList not saved
Description: When using Polyspace for Model Link, additional files can be added to the verification using the option 'AdditionalFileList'. However, this list is not saved in the model properties, so the list is lost when reopening the model. The problem happens with MATLAB version 12a or earlier.
Workaround: Installation instructions for R2012b Polyspace clientLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation folder, referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. By default this is in: Windows: C:\PolyspaceLinux: /usr/local/PolyspaceDownload the .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.For each of the files listed in the .zip file, make a back-up copy of your installed version of the file. To see the contents of the .zip file:Windows: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux: Type in a shell,unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux: Type the in a shell,cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check the binaries have appropriate 'read & execute' permissions.Delete the .zip file from the <POLYSPACE> folder.If you use a more recent version of Polyspace, please contact technical support to request a patch for your version.

Summary: Incorrect word length for numeric type when importing R4.0 AUTOSAR XML containing COMPU-METHOD with LOWER-LIMIT and UPPER-LIMIT
Description: The AUTOSAR importer incorrectly sets the word length of a Simulink numeric type if all of the following conditions are true: The ARXML files being imported use AUTOSAR schema version 4.0. In the ARXML code, an application data type refers to a COMPU-METHOD, of category LINEAR, that has a LOWER-LIMIT and an UPPER-LIMIT. The corresponding implementation data type does not reference a DATA-CONSTR with INTERNAL-CONSTRS.  For example, the following ARXML code shows an application data type, implementation data type, and COMPU-METHOD for which this error occurs. See the comments in the implementation data type, which show where a DATA-CONSTR reference could be supplied, but is not present. <APPLICATION-PRIMITIVE-DATA-TYPE>   <SHORT-NAME>applType</SHORT-NAME>   <CATEGORY>VALUE</CATEGORY>   <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS>      <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-VARIANTS>         <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-CONDITIONAL>            <SW-CALIBRATION-ACCESS>READ-ONLY</SW-CALIBRATION-ACCESS>            <COMPU-METHOD-REF DEST="COMPU-METHOD">            /RootPackage1/CompuMethod/compu1            </COMPU-METHOD-REF>          </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-CONDITIONAL>        </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-VARIANTS>   </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS></APPLICATION-PRIMITIVE-DATA-TYPE><IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE>   <SHORT-NAME>implType1</SHORT-NAME>   <CATEGORY>VALUE</CATEGORY>   <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS>      <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-VARIANTS>         <SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-CONDITIONAL>            <BASE-TYPE-REF DEST="SW-BASE-TYPE">/RootPkg/SwBaseTypes/uint8</BASE-TYPE-REF>            <!-- Missing DATA-CONSTR-REF -->            <!-- <DATA-CONSTR-REF DEST="DATA-CONSTR">/RootPkg/DataConstrs/DC_uint8</DATA-CONSTR-REF> -->         </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-CONDITIONAL>       </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS-VARIANTS>    </SW-DATA-DEF-PROPS></IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE><COMPU-METHOD>   <SHORT-NAME>compu1</SHORT-NAME>   <CATEGORY>LINEAR</CATEGORY>   <COMPU-INTERNAL-TO-PHYS>      <COMPU-SCALES>         <COMPU-SCALE>            <SHORT-LABEL>intToPhys</SHORT-LABEL>            <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">-12800.0</LOWER-LIMIT>            <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">12800.0</UPPER-LIMIT>            <!-- ... -->          </COMPU-SCALE>      </COMPU-SCALES>   </COMPU-INTERNAL-TO-PHYS></COMPU-METHOD>
Workaround: To work around this bug, in the ARXML specification, supply a DATA-CONSTR with INTERNAL-CONSTRS and reference it from the implementation data type.For example, for the ARXML code above, you could uncomment the DATA-CONSTR-REF and supply the DATA-CONSTR as follows:<DATA-CONSTR>   <SHORT-NAME>DC_uint8</SHORT-NAME>   <DATA-CONSTR-RULES>      <DATA-CONSTR-RULE>         <INTERNAL-CONSTRS>            <LOWER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">0</LOWER-LIMIT>            <UPPER-LIMIT INTERVAL-TYPE="CLOSED">255</UPPER-LIMIT>         </INTERNAL-CONSTRS>      </DATA-CONSTR-RULE>   </DATA-CONSTR-RULES></DATA-CONSTR>

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable custom storage classes for signal objects may lead to incorrect code
Description: The generated code may be incorrect when all of the following are true: Signal objects are specified as Reusable  custom storage classes. Signals are reused at the model root inputs and outputs. In the Configuration parameters dialog box, on the Model Referencing  pane, the check box Pass fixed-size scalar root inputs by value for code generation  is selected.
Workaround: Do one of the following:In the Configuration parameters dialog box, on the Model Referencing pane, clear the check box Pass fixed-size scalar root inputs by value for code generation.Specify the signal as any custom storage class other than Reusable.

Summary: The function name evaluatecluster in R2013b release notes should be evalclusters.
Description: The first sentence of the third bullet in R2013b release notes states: "evaluatecluster  function for estimating the optimal number of clusters in data " The name of the new function is evalclusters  not evaluatecluster . So it should say: "evalclusters  function for estimating the optimal number of clusters in data ". The name evaluatecluster  appears two more times in the release note. There is no function named evaluateclusters  in Statistics Toolbox.

Summary: Verification block settings not in effect after saving model
Description: In the Signal Builder dialog box, the Verification Manager shows settings for Model Verification blocks in your model. When you change these verification block settings and then save your model, the updated verification block settings do not take effect. This can occur if you have multiple test cases in the Signal Builder and if you save your model from inside the Signal Builder. For your verification block settings to take effect, after you save your changes to the verification block settings, close your model and reopen it. ? ?

Summary: MATLAB? can crash during MEX function generation when using (u)int64 with integer overflow support disabled.
Description: If integer overflow support is disabled, and you generate a MEX function from MATLAB?  code that uses int64  or uint64  in arithmetic operations, MATLAB can crash. This behavior occurs with the uminus  (i.e. -x ), mrdivide( / ) , and mldivide( \ )  operators and with functions that call these operators.The resulting stack trace contains a line like:classic/cg_type.cpp:2433: <unknown>: Assertion `sizeof(int) * 8 >= (size_t)nBits' failed.
Workaround: Check that integer overflow support is enabled when using MATLAB Coder to generate a MEX function for code that uses int64 or uint64 variables in arithmetic operations.To enable integer overflow support:In a project, on the Project Settings dialog box Speed tab, select the Saturate on integer overflow check box.At the command line, set the SaturateOnIntegerOverflow configuration parameter to true.

Relax the constraints to find a feasible solution. This error can occur when you trim the model using the findop  command or the MPC Design tool.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code from second-generation models with gimbal-based joints in kinematic loops may fail to compile
Description: Code that you generate from a second-generation model may compile incorrectly. The compilation error occurs if: The model contains a gimbal-based joint block (i.e., a joint block with three revolute primitives). The gimbal-based joint block occurs in a kinematic loop. SimMechanics selects the gimbal-based joint as the cut joint during assembly.  This error may affect you if you generate code directly or if you run a simulation in rapid accelerator mode. The exact error messages depend on the compiler and platform, but they should indicate that: The error occurs in a file with the suffix _assembly.c. 'neDiagMgr' is an undeclared identifier.  Gimbal-based joint blocks Affected joint blocks include: Gimbal Joint Bearing Joint Bushing Joint  Kinematic Loops Your model has a kinematic loop if it contains: A closed circuit of rigid bodies and joints. An open sequence of rigid bodies and joints that begins and ends at the World frame.  For example, a double pendulum has no kinematic loops. A four-bar mechanism has one. Cut Joints During assembly, SimMechanics cuts one joint per kinematic loop and replaces it with an equivalent constraint. The cut-joint selection occurs automatically and favors joints with many degrees of freedom. Because of this bias, gimbal joints are often the cut joints in kinematic loops that have them.
Workaround: You can avoid the code compilation error using one of three workarounds.1. Gimbal-Spherical SubstitutionThe simplest solution is to avoid gimbal-based joint blocks in kinematic loops. SimMechanics provides alternative blocks, based on spherical primitives, with the same degrees of freedom. Consider using these blocks instead. The table shows the gimbal-spherical block correspondence.Gimbal-Based Joint BlockSpherical-Based Joint BlockGimbal JointSpherical JointBearing JointTelescoping Joint**Bushing Joint6-DOF Joint** When substituting a Bearing Joint block with a Telescoping Joint block, you must reverse the base and follower frame ports. This is because the prismatic axes of the two blocks are different: prismatic motion occurs along the base frame Z axis when using the Bearing Joint block, but along the follower frame Z axis when using the Telescoping Joint block. Reversing frame ports negates joint angles.Use this workaround if the non-uniform kinematics of a gimbal-based joint are irrelevant to your model. Note that sensing and actuation work differently in the two types of blocks. In addition, spherical joint primitives do not currently support prescribed motion inputs.2. Compound Joint ModelingAn alternative solution is to model the gimbal-based joint as a compound sequence of simpler joints. This approach better preserves the gimbal kinematics as well as its actuation and sensing characteristics. You can approximate a gimbal joint by connecting in series:Three Revolute Joint blocks, orOne Revolute Joint block and one Universal Joint block.To approximate a bearing joint, add to the previous sequences a Prismatic Joint block. To approximate a bushing joint, add a Cartesian Joint block.You must insert a small mass between each joint pair in the previous sequences. Failure to do so results in a simulation error. To minimize the adverse impact on model dynamics, keep the mass values as small as possible. Note, however, that very small values can introduce stiffness to the system, slowing model simulation. Insert Rigid Transform blocks between the joint pairs as needed to achieve the kinematics of the original joint.3. Code ModificationA final approach is to manually remove the compilation errors from the generated code. This approach leaves the original gimbal-based joints intact. You must, however, manually compile the code. This approach is therefore ill-suited for running a model in rapid accelerator mode.To remove the compilation error from the generated code:Open the generated code file with the suffix _assembly.c. This is the file with the reported compilation errors.Search for C statements of the form { return sm_ssci_recordRunTimeError(...); }, where "..." refers to an argument list that includes error messages related to gimbal lock.Delete the C statements, leaving only the curly braces: { }Removing these statements eliminates the compilation errors. Adverse effects are rare. You may encounter simulation issues if model assembly places the gimbal-based joints in a gimbal lock configuration. You can avoid gimbal lock by adding or adjusting the position state targets for the gimbal-based joints.

Summary: Constellation Diagram block generates incorrect results for oversampled inputs whose length is not an integer multiple of the samples per symbol parameter.
Description: The Constellation Diagram block displays incorrect constellation points for oversampled inputs (samples per symbol parameter > 1), which are either scalars or vectors whose length is not an integer multiple of the samples per symbol parameter.
Workaround: Buffer the input signal to a length which is an integer multiple of the samples per symbol parameter.

Summary: mpc, review, display, and MPC Design Tool fail for plant with internal delays that cannot be converted to I/O delays
Description: When a plant contains internal delays that cannot be converted into I/O delays, the mpc , review , and display  commands generate an error. The error includes the following: The "totaldelay" command cannot be used for models with internal delays. A similar error occurs when you import such a plant into the MPC Design Tool. This error includes the following: Cannot access the "ioDelay" property. This model has internal delays that cannot be expressed as I/O delays.
Workaround: Discretize the plant model using the c2d command. Then, replace all the discrete model delays with poles at the origin using the absorbDelay command. Use the resulting plant model to create the controller.For example:sys_err = feedback(tf(1,[1 1],'InputDelay',1),pid(1,1));Ts = 0.1;sysd = absorbDelay(c2d(sys_err,Ts));MPCobj = mpc(sysd);

Summary: Reading a file using textscan with text mode can return incorrect output
Description: The textscan  function can return incorrect output when reading a file that was opened with text mode file access in fopen . Paticularly, it might return the wrong file position which might result in incorrect data output of subsequent calls to textscan .  The condition that causes this bug is difficult to predict since it requires a newline character at the end of an internal buffer.  For example, create a file with a newline character:fileID = fopen('newline.txt', 'w'); InternalBufferSize = 980; str = [repmat('a', 1, InternalBufferSize -1) sprintf('\r\n') 'a']; fwrite(fileID , str); fclose(fileID );  If you open the file in binary mode using fopen , and then read the file using textscan , the textscan  function returns the correct output:fileID = fopen('newline.txt', 'r');[~, position] = textscan(fileID , '%s') fclose(fileID );  However, if you open the file in text mode using fopen , the textscan  function returns the incorrect position output: fileID2= fopen('newline.txt', 'rt'); [~, position2] = textscan(fileID2, '%s')fclose(fileID2);  Subsequent calls to textscan might return incorrect data.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initialization of a persistent variable in an if-isempty block when the value assigned to the persistent variable depends on a function input
Description: If the value assigned to a persistent variable in an if-isempty  block depends on a function input parameter, the generated code can assign an incorrect value to the persistent variable. This example produces incorrect results: function res = twoFuncs(n)  if(n > 0)    res = foo('versionA');else    res = foo('versionB');endend function y = foo(str) persistent x;if(strcmp(str, 'versionA'))    if(isempty(x))        x = -100;    endelse    if(isempty(x))        x = 200;    endendy = x;end
Workaround: Modify the function so that the initial value assigned to the persistent variable does not depend on a function input parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Assignment to a persistent variable in an if-isempty  block can cause incorrect generated code.
Description: The code generation software can produce an incorrect value for a persistent variable when the following conditions are true: You assign a value to the persistent variable before assigning a value to that variable inside an if-isempty  block. The input to the isempty  function is another persistent variable.   The following example produces incorrect code: function z = foo  persistent x;  persistent y;  y = -999;  if(isempty(x))    y = 100;    x = 1;  end  z = y;end
Workaround: Modify the code so that you do not assign a value to a persistent variable before assigning a value to that variable in an if-isempty block.?

Summary: Simulink-based BERTool simulations stop after the first Eb/No point if the model's StopFcn contains a comment as its last line.
Description: If the model's StopFcn contains a comment as its last line, and it is run in a BERTool session, the model will run only the first Eb/No point. If the last line of StopFcn contains executable code, the model will run all the Eb/No points. If the StopFcn is empty, the model will run all the Eb/No points.
Workaround: Make sure that the last line of the StopFcn is not a comment line.

Summary: Specifying a MATLAB structure for the parameter Value in a Constant block may generate an error even if a valid bus type is specified as the Output data type
Description: Updating or simulating a model containing a Constant block with the following settings: Constant value : MATLAB structure Output data type : Bus: valid bus-type  results in the following error message:  The data type specified for the block 'modelname/Constant' is 'non-bus type' and the value specified for the parameter 'Value' of the block is a MATLAB structure. MATLAB structures are supported only when bus types are explicitly specified. Set the data type explicitly to a matching bus object or specify an expression which resolves to a bus object.
Workaround: As a workaround:Close and reopen the model.Before updating or simulating the model, identify the Constant blocks that use MATLAB structures as their Constant value parameters.Check that the Output data type parameter for these blocks are set as Bus: <object name>, where object name is a valid bus object name.Update or simulate the model.

Summary: vpasolve function always returns solutions sorted in alphabetical order of the names of the variables to solve for.
Description: Expected behavior : If you explicitly specify variables to solve for, then vpasolve  returns solutions in the order of the specified variables. For example, in release 2012b: >> syms x y z;>> [solx,soly,solz] = vpasolve([cos(x)==0,sin(y)==0,cos(z)==0], [x,y,z]);>> double([cos(solx),sin(soly),cos(solz)])ans =   1.0e-37 *    0.0001    0.8638    0.0000 Issue : vpasolve  always returns solutions sorted in alphabetical order of the names of the specified variables. For example, in release 2012b: >> syms x y z;>> [solz,solx,soly] = vpasolve([cos(x)==0,sin(y)==0,cos(z)==0], [z,x,y]);>> double([cos(solx),sin(soly),cos(solz)])ans =   -1.0000   -1.0000    0.0000
Workaround: When you assign solutions returned by vpasolve to variables, put these variables in alphabetical order. For example, in release 2012b:>> syms x y z;>> [solx,soly,solz] = vpasolve([cos(x)==0,sin(y)==0,cos(z)==0], [x,y,z]);>> double([cos(solx),sin(soly),cos(solz)])ans =   1.0e-37 *    0.0001    0.8638    0.0000

Summary: License error generating Data Range Specification
Description: When trying to generate a Data Range Specification (DRS) configuration using the Generate  button in the Polyspace DRS Configuration interface, the generation fails with a license error such as: Checking license ...  Error: No Polyspace Code Prover license available  | License checkout failed.  License Manager Error -5  Cannot find a license for PolySpace_Server_C_CPP.  ?
Workaround: Launch a verification without DRS. After the compilation phase completes, a template DRS file called drs-template.xml is generated in the results folder.Copy the drs-template.xml file near configuration file. Summary: The trainCascadeObjectDetector function fails to create the output XML file on a Windows 7 operating system.
Description: If the Windows 7 current directory has the 'system ' attribute set to true, the trainCascadeObjectDetector  function fails to produce the output XML file. You can verify the 'system ' attribute for the folder by using the fileattrib  function in MATLAB. The trainCascadeObjectDetector  function runs through a training session, and then returns an error at the very end of the session. The Windows 7 environment requires appropriate user account privilages  to create a new file in the system folder. Thus, the error occurs, and the creation of the output XML file fails when the function attempts to produce it.
Workaround: Use the fileattrib function in MATLAB to check the 'system' attribute for the current directory. If the attribute is set to true, navigate to a different directory before calling the trainCascadeObjectDetector function.To avoid the problem in the future, download the attached trainCascadeObjectDetectorPatch.zip file and follow these steps:Uncompress the zip file.Copy the trainCascadeObjectDetector.xml file into the <MATLABROOT>\resources\vision\en folder. <MATLABROOT> can be obtained by running the matlabroot function.Copy the trainCascadeObjectDetector.m file into the <MATLABROOT>\toolbox\vision\vision folder.If prompted whether to replace existing files, click yes.After copying the files, restart MATLAB.After this change, the trainCascadeObjectDetector function returns an error immediately, if the current directory is a 'system' folder.

Summary: Model containing inlined atomic subsystems that were previously nonreusable functions may fail to build ??
Description: A model containing atomic subsystems with the Function packaging  parameter set to Inline  may fail to build if the atomic subsystems previously had the following settings: The Function with separate data  parameter was selected. The Function packaging  parameter was set to Nonreusable function .? The Function interface  parameter was set to Allow Arguments .  If the model fails to build, the user gets the following error message: CGIR assertion 'aCastExpr->isReshaping() || aCastExpr->isDmtConversion() || aCastExpr->isNoop()' failed
Workaround: The bug occurs because in the background, the inlined subsystem has the previous nonreusable function settings.In the MATLAB Command Window, run the following script to find all instances of this subsystem block setting and correct it.?blks = ?find_system(bdroot, 'Regexp', 'on','type', 'block', 'BlockType', 'SubSystem', ...? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? 'RTWSystemCode', '^[IA][nu][lt][io]', 'FunctionInterfaceSpec', 'Allow arguments');?for idx=1:length(blks)? set_param(blks{idx}, 'FunctionInterfaceSpec', 'void_void');end?

Summary: Simulink terminates abruptly when updating a model with function-call subsystem driven by unmatched From block.
Description: Your model is subject to this bug when all of the following conditions are true: There is a function-call subsystem that is driven by at least two function-call signals through a Mux block. At least one of the above function-call signals originate from an unmatched From block. Specifically, there is no matching Goto block with the same tag.
Workaround: Use either of the following possible workarounds to avoid the abrupt termination:Set Model Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity > Unconnected line: to 'error'. With this setting, Simulink will throw an error message on the unmatched From block instead of terminating abruptly. This diagnostic will also error out if there is an unconnected line in the model.Call the attached function, FindUnmatchedFrom, from the InitFcn callback of the model with the name of the model as an argument. This will cause Simulink to error out upon finding an unmatched From block.

Summary: Memory leaks during code inspection
Description: Simulink Code Inspector uses an internal data object to communicate with the Simulink model.  The data object memory is not freed after code inspection, causing memory leaks for each subsequent  inspection. ?
Workaround: Consider restarting MATLAB? after mulitple runs of the Simulink Code Inspector.

Summary: Overflow not detected in type conversion from signed to longer unsigned
Description: Design error detection analysis for integer overflow does not detect potential overflow in a Data Type Conversion block that converts a signed type to an unsigned type with longer word length. For example, conversion from int16  to uint32  can overflow for negative values in the range of int16 . If your model has a Data Type Conversion block that implements this conversion, when you run design error detection analysis for integer overflow, the analysis completes normally but does not highlight or report results for objectives on the Data Type Conversion block. If overflow occurs in simulation, depending on the model configuration parameter setting for Diagnostics  > Data Validity  > Signals  > Detect overflow , a warning or an error can appear in the Command Window.

Summary: Problems using copyobj with surface handles from geoshow
Description: If you create a surface handle using geoshow  and then attempt to use copyobj  to copy the surface to a new axes, the resultant figure, in some instances, is not correct. You may see that part of the surface is missing. Additionally, if you delete the copied handle, then both the original and copied surfaces are deleted.

Summary: Using a global data store in an Export-Function model causes MATLAB to crash in certain cases
Description: Performing an update-diagram operation on a model with access to a global data store and containing a Model block that references an Export-Function model will cause MATLAB to crash in the following two similar cases: Case 1: The model has at least three levels of model hierarchy (i.e. a "top-level" model contains a Model block referencing a "middle-level" model which in turn contains a Model block referencing a "bottom-level" model). Updating the top-level model will cause MATLAB to crash if all the following conditions are true: A?global data store ?is used. The middle-level model is an Export-Function model. In the bottom-level model, there are Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks accessing the global data store. In the middle-level model, there are no Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks that access the global data store.  An example of this case is shown in the attached model mTopMdl_G978240.slx. Case 2: The model once again has at least three levels of model hierarchy. Performing an update-diagram operation on the top-level model will cause MATLAB to crash if all of the following conditions are true: A?global data store ?is used. The middle-level model is not an Export-Function model. The bottom-level model is an Export-Function model. In the bottom-level model, there are Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks accessing the global data store. In the middle-level model, there are no Data Store Read or Data Store Write blocks that access the global data store.  An example of this case is shown in attached model mTopMdl_G985586.slx.
Workaround: Modify your model such that at least one of the conditions mentioned above becomes false.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening certain models with a saved Simulink Scope
Description: Loading or opening certain models containing the Simulink Scope can crash MATLAB. The problematic models have the NumInputPorts parameter serialized incorrectly in the Scope's BlockParameterDefaults section of the MDL/SLX file.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, if your model is in MDL format, open the problematic MDL file as text, and remove the NumInputPorts parameter from the Scope's BlockParameterDefaults section. This workaround is not available for SLX files.

Summary: The Featured Example commqpskrxhdl runs extremely slowly.
Description: Although the model is in Accelerator mode, the sluggish performance is because the two HDL Optimized NCO blocks are set to "Interpreted execution" mode. They should simulate in "Code generation" mode.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Generated code of Constant block might pass ouput to function as incorrect pointer type
Description: In the generated code for a model, the variable corresponding to the output of a Constant block might be passed to a function as a pointer of the wrong data type. This might happen if all of the following are true:  In the Constant block, the parameter defining the Constant value is a tunable parameter (for example, the configuration parameter checkbox, Inline parameters , is selected, and the Storage class  of the parameter is not Auto . You explicitly define the data type of that parameter, and that data type does not match the the data type of the Constant block output. The Constant block is connected to a block that is represented in the code as a function call. For example, a reusable subsystem, MATLAB Function block, or Model block.  That function call passes the variable corresponding to the Constant block's output by address as a function call argument. In such cases, the compiler produces a warning similar to the following: foo.c:37:3: warning: passing argument 1 of 'y' from incompatible pointer type [enabled by default] : in file included from foo.h:33:0, : from foo.c:18: : referenced_model_includes/foo_bot.h:27:13: note: expected 'const real32_t *' but argument is of type 'int32_t *'
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:Specify the data type of the tunable parameter as the value of the Constant block parameter Output data type.Specify the Constant block parameter Output data type as Inherit: Inherit from 'Constant value'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C code might not compile for models with variant subsystem block
Description: Generated C code might not compile for models with a variant subsystem block if the following conditions exist: On the Configuration Parameters  > Optimization > Signals and Parameters  pane, the value of Maximum stack size (bytes)  is very low or zero. The variant subsystem contains only blocks that do not store state.  In this case, the software might generate references to non-existing data structures.
Workaround: On the Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Signals and Parameters pane, increase the value of the Maximum stack size (bytes) parameter.If this is not possible, on the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface pane, try selecting the Combine signal/state structures check box.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The generated code gives a wrong answer when the Weighted Sample Time Math block is inside a triggered subsystem and its parameter Weight value is not of data type double.
Description: When the Weighted Sample Time Math block is inside a triggered subsystem, the Weight value  parameter value may be incorrectly cast to a wrong value of data type double , even thought it is set to another data type. As a result, the value of the weight parameter is wrong in the generated code. This does not affect simulation.
Workaround: Change the data type of Weight value in the Weighted Sample Time Math block to double.

Summary: Code inspection Failed to verify status for compatible models containing Subsystem blocks with block parameter MinAlgLoopOccurrences set to on.
Description: Simulink Code Inspector compatibility check Check usage of Ports and Subsystems blocks > Check Subsystem blocks   fails to detect when subsystem block parameter Minimize algebraic loop occurrences   is set to on . To be compatible with code inspection, Minimize algebraic loop occurrences  must be set to off . The command-line form of the parameter is MinAlgLoopOccurrences .
Workaround: For the Subsystems in your model, set block parameter Minimize algebraic loop occurrences (MinAlgLoopOccurrences) to off.

Summary: The MuPAD partfrac function does not react properly to its options when the input contains floating-point numbers.
Description: The MuPAD partfrac  function does not react to its options when the input contains floating-point numbers. For example,  partfrac(x/(x^2 - 4.0), x, List) produces 0.5 / (x + 2.0) + 0.5 / (x - 2.0) This is correct only if the option List  is not specified. Without a floating-point entry, the option is used properly. partfrac(x/(x^2 - 4), x, List) produces the expected result [1/2, 1/2], [x - 2, x + 2]
Workaround: Replace floating-point numbers by rational numbers using numeric::rationalize. Call partfrac with the rationalized argument, and then apply float to the result. For example:f := x / (x^2 - 4.0);r := numeric::rationalize(f);[numerators, denominators] := float([partfrac(r, x, List)])

Summary: Build for a model hierarchy with Model blocks can fail when changing a tunable parameter to nontunable using a build entry hook
Description: Building a model that references another model fails if all  of the following conditions apply: The Model block in the parent model has Simulation Mode  set to Accelerator . The referenced model has a block that uses a MATLAB workspace variable (for example, param ) that is of type Simulink.Parameter  and has a non-Auto storage class (which makes it a tunable parameter). The parent model changes the storage class of param  to Auto  during the build entry hook, which turns it into a nontunable parameter.  The build failure displays an error message similar to this: The parameter 'param' was declared by referenced model 'child' as being tunable. The current definition of the parameter declares it to be nontunable. This is not supported. Change the parameter so that it is declared to be tunable.
Workaround: In the parent model, set the Simulation Mode of the Model block to Normal.

Summary: Status remains 'populating database' on Polyspace Metrics webpage
Description: When you upload results from the Polyspace Environment to the Polyspace Metrics Web Interface, the upload is successful. However, if you try to access the results from the Polyspace Metrics webpage, the status does not change from 'populating database'. If you check the polyspaced.log file (located by default in /var/Polyspace on the machine hosting Polyspace Metrics), you will see a messages such as: date , hours : Warning: *** Unable to create folder "/tmp/Polyspace/434B_1374670771". Retrying...System error Permission denied (acces: Permission denied)*** Unable to create folder "/tmp/Polyspace/434B_1374670771". Retrying...System error Permission denied (acces: Permission denied)*** Unable to create folder "/tmp/Polyspace/434B_1374670771". Retrying...System error Permission denied (acces: Permission denied)*** Unable to create folder "/tmp/Polyspace/434B_1374670771". ... 'polyspace-rl-manager' GUI has created a temporary folder '/tmp/Polyspace' with 755 rights whereas it should be 777.
Workaround: Change the access rights of the /tmp/Polyspace folder to 777.

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Simulation and code generation mismatch for saturation block when input is NaN and upper limit is Inf or lower limit is -Inf .
Description: For the saturation block, suppose the input is NaN  and the upper limit of the saturation block is Inf . In simulation, the output gives NaN , which is the desired result. However, in Accelerator mode or the generated code, the output will be the lower limit. This results in a mismatch between simulation and code generation. A similar problem will occur if the lower limit is -Inf . In simulation, the output gives NaN , which is the desired result. However, in Accelerator mode or the generated code, the output will be the upper limit. There is no problem if the upper limit is Inf  and the lower limit is -Inf  at the same time.

Summary: Using a sparse matrix in containers.Map has unexpected results
Description: Constructing a containers.Map  object using a sparse matrix as a Key or Value argument results in unexpected values and/or keys.   Example 1: >> A = speye(3) % contains 9 elements>> m = containers.Map(1:9,A(:))>> m(6) % Expect the same value as A(6)ans =     -1.3684900068106e-231>> A(6)ans =   All zero sparse: 1-by-1 This does not affect the use of sparse matrices with the 'ValueType' = 'any'  input argument. Example 2: >> m = containers.Map({'key1','key2'},{speye(2),speye(3)});>> m('key2')ans =   (1,1)        1   (2,2)        1   (3,3)        1
Workaround: To use the elements in a sparse matrix as keys or values in a containers.Map object, first use the full function on the sparse array.>> A = speye(3) % contains 9 elements>> m = containers.Map(1:9,full(A(:)));>> m(6)ans =     0>> m(5)ans =     1

Description: Models that contain the Geodetic to Geocentric Latitude, Geocentric to Geodetic Latitude, and LLA to ECEF Position blocks generate code that takes slightly longer than normal to run.

Description: When blocks are selected in Simulink, the following copy-paste actions may take several minutes to complete: Keyboard accelerators for copy and paste (Ctrl-C  and Ctrl-V  on Windows and Linux, Command-C  and Command-V  on Mac)  Menu options Edit->Copy  and Edit-> Paste   This is especially noticeable when working with large models and when copying/pasting large numbers of blocks.

Summary: Deleting a subsystem may cause Simulink to crash
Description: Under certain circumstances, Simulink may crash while cutting, or deleting subsystems.

Summary: Code generation error for colon function with single and double endpoints.
Description: Suppose a  is double and c  is single (or a  is single and c  is double). If you generate code for a:d:c , you can receive an error like ??? Class mismatch (double ~= single).The class to the left is the class of the left-hand side of the assignment.
Workaround: Cast a or c so that they both have the same type. Suppose a is double and c is single. The generated code for single(a):d:c uses single-precision arithmetic.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The rem function of the Math Function block generates incorrect code when the second input is set to infinity
Description: Incorrect code is generated by the Math Function block when the following conditions are true: Math Function block with the Function  parameter set to rem  The checkbox Support non-finite numbers  under Simulation  > Model Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Interface  is selected Code is generated through a GRT or ERT target OR Simulation is run with Mode  under Simulation  set to either Normal , Software-in-the-loop (SIL) , Processsor-in-the-Loop (PIL) , or External  The first input to the block is a finite number The second input to the block is infinity

Summary: Examples for "Parallel Simulations Using Parfor" do not open properly.
Description: When using MATLAB Help, some examples for "Parallel Simulations Using Parfor" do not open properly when using the "Open this example" link. They are: 1.Test-Case Sweep 2.Parameter Sweep in Normal Mode 3.Parameter Sweep in Rapid Accelerator Mode Clicking on the "Open this Example" link does not open the model 'sldemo_suspn_3dof'. Instead, Simulink displays the following message in a dialog box:"filename.m file does not exist. Do you want to create it?"where filename.m can refer to one of the following 3 files: 1.sldemo_parfor_paramsweep_syspn_raccel.m 2.sldemo_parfor_paramsweep_syspn.m 3.sldemo_parfor_testcase_suspn.m
Workaround: To work around this issue, open the model by typing sldemo_suspn_3dof in the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Some regular expressions with lookbehind assertions take exponential time to solve
Description: Some regular expressions with lookbehind assertions take exponential time to solve. Lookbehind assertions are either (?<=test)  or (?<!test) , where test  is a subpattern to solve.  The lookbehind assertions that cause an issue have either a character set or an alternation pattern as the subpattern. Consider these examples: regexp(str, '(?<=[abcd])efg')  or regexp(str, '(?<=a|b|c|d)efg')  Both examples have a lookbehind assertion that causes regexp  to take a long time to solve the regular expression.
Workaround: Restate the lookbehind assertion so it does not contain a set or alternation.  Consecutive lookbehind assertions can serve the same purpose.Consider the first example:regexp(str, '(?<=[abcd])efg')The lookbehind assertion can be changed to:regexp(str, '((?<=a)|(?<=b)|(?<=c)|(?<=d))efg')A single range is also acceptable, when it suits the pattern, so the assertion can instead be changed to:regexp(str, '(?<=[a-d])efg')Positive lookbehind assertions need to be concatenated using alternation, as above.  Negative lookbehind assertions need to be listed consecutively without alternation.  In the following example:regexp(str, '(?<![hijk])efg')The lookbehind assertion can be changed to:regexp(str, '(?<!h)(?<!i)(?<!j)(?<!k)efg')

Summary: Some defects are lost when the batch mode is used
Description: When an analysis is done in batch mode, some defects, found in non-batch mode, are lost. The exact list of concerned defects is: Static memory - 'Unreliable cast of function pointer' and 'Unreliable cast of pointer' Programming - all Data-flow - 'Variable shadowing', 'Missing or invalid return statement', and 'Code deactivated by constant false condition' Other - 'Large pass-by-value argument' and 'Format string specifiers and arguments mismatch'
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\Program Files\MATLAB or in /usr/local/MATLAB on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB_ROOT> in subsequent steps.Close the Polyspace User Interface.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB_ROOT> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <MATLAB_ROOT>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB_ROOT> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <MATLAB_ROOT>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB_ROOT> folder.Reopen the Polyspace verification environment.

Summary: The MATLAB Engine interface can wrongly set Unix errno to ESPIPE (29).
Description: When sending a variable to a MATLAB Engine, using engPutVariable, the Unix error state, errno, might be set to ESPIPE (numerical value 29) indicating an unsupported operation on a pipe.
Workaround: Ignore, or clear, errno. No data is lost from the engPutVariable operation.

Summary: Referenced model containing a look-up table does not build correctly for Real-Time Windows Target
Description: In Real-Time Windows Target, when you compile a referenced model containing blocks such as a Look-Up Table block, the compilation fails and produces an error message.
Workaround: As a workaround:Log into your computer using an account with Administrator privileges.Look at the list of available workaround files, named according to the release they support.Select and download the workaround file for your release.Locate the directory of the downloaded file. This directory is referred to as <DOWNLOAD> in subsequent steps.Type matlabroot to locate your MATLAB installation. This directory is referred to as <MATLABROOT> in subsequent steps.Navigate to <DOWNLOAD> and open the downloaded file.Extract the contents of the file to <MATLABROOT>.January 14, 2014: attachment_972243_12b_2014-02-14.zip: works on R2012b onlyJanuary 14, 2014: attachment_972243_13a_2014-02-14.zip: works on R2013a onlyJanuary 14, 2014: attachment_972243_13b_2014-02-14.zip: works on R2013b only

Summary: Assertion occurs when replacing code for fixed-point operation ?
Description: The following assertion can occur when the code generator uses a code replacement library to replace a fixed-point operation: CGIR assertion 'T::isa((const CG::Type*)aType)' This assertion occurs when both of the following conditions exist: The specified code replacement library includes an entry that replaces a fixed-point operation. The size of the data type specified for the output argument in the entry's implementation specification differs from that of the conceptual specification. For example, the assertion occurs if an entry's conceptual specification defines output argument y1  to be of type int16  while the implementation specification defines y1  to be of type int32 . ?
Workaround: Modify the code replacement entry such that the data type size for the output argument is the same for the conceptual and implementation specifications.

Summary: Extra space around map created with the usamap function
Description: Due to an unintended change in R2013a, the usamap  function no longer tightens the axes limits and it no longer leaves the axes XLimMode  and YLimMode  in a 'manual'  (rather than 'auto' ) state.
Workaround: You can work around this unintended change by following your call to usamap with a call to tightmap.

Summary: Opening the Web view of a protected model that uses fixed-point data checks out the Fixed-Point Designer license
Description: The license checkout behavior of protected and unprotected models with fixed-point data is inconsistent. Opening the Web view of a protected model with fixed-point data checks out the Fixed-Point Designer License.  Opening an unprotected model with fixed-point data does not  check out the Fixed-Point Designer license. When you use the unprotected model for simulation or code generation, then Simuluink checks out the license.

Summary: Models with I/O interrupts crash xPC Target (Simulink Real-Time) on target computers running the Intel i7 and some i3 CPUs
Description: On target computers featuring Intel i7 and some i3 CPUs, models using I/O interrupts can cause xPC Target (Simulink Real-Time) to crash. Typically, the exception message on the target screen under text mode shows: ...... ...... Code: 0F AE 29......
Workaround: Contact MathWorks technical support to get R2012b, R2013a, or R2013b patches.

Summary: PBS and Torque clusters cannot be used with the findResource-based  interface in R2013a.
Description: PBS and Torque scheduler types cannot be used with the findResource -based  interface in MATLAB R2013a and later. Any attempt to submit a job reports an error in the MATLAB command window.
Workaround: Use the new parcluster-based interface to access PBS and Torque clusters in MATLAB R2013a and later.

Summary: Incorrect output when executing the ccode and fortran commands on symbolic expressions containing 1/cot(anyX). This also applies to the MuPAD functions generate::C and generate::fortran.
Description: When the expression contains 1/cot(anyX) , the ccode  and fortran  commands return incorrect results: syms anyX;m = 1/cot(anyX);ccode(m)ans =   t0 = 1.0/1.0/tan(anyX);fortran(m)ans =       t0 = 1.0D0/1.0D0/tan(anyX)
Workaround: Rewrite all occurrences of 1/cot(anyX) in the expression to tan(anyX) before generating code. In MATLAB R2012a and later usesyms anyX;m = 1/cot(anyX);m = rewrite(m, 'tan')m =tan(anyX)ccode(m)ans =   t0 = tan(x);fortran(m)ans =       t0 = tan(anyX)In MATLAB 2011b and earlier, use syms anyX;m = 1/cot(anyX);m = feval(symengine, 'rewrite', m, 'tan')m =tan(anyX)ccode(m)ans =   t0 = tan(x);fortran(m)ans =       t0 = tan(anyX)

Description: If you create a fittype , cfit , or sfit  using an anonymous function, save it and then load it, then you see errors like the following when you try to plot or evaluate it: Error using cfit/plot (line 111)Error evaluating CFIT function:Error while trying to evaluate CFIT model: obj:Error while trying to evaluate FITTYPE function obj:Argument must contain a string or function_handle. Summary: Annotation object position not settable as a point using Simulink.Annotation object in R2013b
Description: To support rich text annotation, the Simulink.Annotation  property Position  is a rectangle. In R2013b, you cannot set the Position  parameter to be a point. However, R2013b does not use the right and bottom members of the passed rectangle. Simulink calculates the width and the height of the annotation based on the annotation contents.
Workaround: Use one of these two workaroundsUse set_param() instead of accessing the member of the Simulink.Annotation object. For example:note = getSelectedAnnotations(gcs);set_param(note.handle,'Position','[100 100]');Pass a rectangle when accessing the member of the Simulink.Annotation object. The right and the bottom can be any values but greater than or equal to the left and the top respectively. For example:note = getSelectedAnnotations(gcs);note.position=[100 100 100 100];?

Summary: Confusing error message from coder.cstructname  for entry-point inputs containing nested structures
Description: Suppose the entry-point  function my_function  has two structure inputs whose names in the generated code are specified using coder.cstructname:  function my_function(s1, s2) coder.cstructname(s1, 'S1'); coder.cstructname(s2, 'S2');  Suppose that you compile  my_function  as follows: codegen -args { struct('s1field', struct('s2field', 0)), struct('s2field', 0) } my_function  You receive the following error message: ??? The name 'S1' has already been defined using a different type. Was defined as 'struct S1 {s1field: struct struct_T {s2field: double}}'; tried to redefine it as 'struct S1 {s1field: struct S2 {s2field: double}}'  You receive this error because you are mixing unnamed structures with named structures and there's a type dependency between the arguments. s1.s1field  has the same type as s2 , but the code generation software processes the type of input s1.s1field  before it assigns the name S2  to input s2 .
Workaround: Do not use unnamed structures. For the above example, explicitly specify that S2 is the name of the nested structure s1.s1field:coder.cstructname(s1, 'S1');coder.cstructname(s1.s1field, 'S2');coder.cstructname(s2, 'S2');??

Summary: Opening a MATLAB file fails with the message "There was a problem sending the command to the program."
Description: Double-clicking on a MATLAB file (.M) from within the Windows File Explorer causes the following Windows error message: "There was a problem sending the command to the program."
Workaround: Start MATLAB first, and then open desired file from within MATLAB.

Summary: Segmentation violation when get_param is used with empty object
Description: Segmentation violation and subsequent MATLAB crash occur when you use get_param  with an empty object. For example, the following get_param  invocation can cause MATLAB to crash: get_param(cell(1,1), 'xyz')

Summary: Saving a Simulink library model with a Stateflow chart containing Simulink functions can result in an error
Description: Saving a Simulink library model with a Stateflow chart containing Simulink functions can cause command line errors and prevent the library from being saved. This error occurs with this workflow: A link to the library chart containing the Simulink function is instantiated in a non-library model. The non-library model is simulated. The non-library model is closed. The library model is saved.  The following error appears in the MATLAB command window and the library model is not saved: Bad object handle (###) at index 1. Error in parameter number 2. Error using Stateflow.SLINSF.SimfcnMan/syncPortProps/syncPortPropsHelper/shouldDoMalInheritedPortCheck (line ###) Object parameter contains an invalid handle.
Workaround: Do not close the non-library model before to saving the library model.

Summary: Property Inspector displays incorrect properties when accessing multiple objects
Description: When viewing an array containing more than one object in the Property Inspector, some properties do not display or update correctly. For example, some properties do not display correctly if you select more than one graphics object and then open the Property Inspector. Specifically: Callback properties such as ButtonDownFcn  display the string, Multi , even when the callback property is identical for all the selected objects. When you edit callback properties such as ButtonDownFcn , the modified value is not displayed without first clicking on another cell. Moreover, the change does not update the callback property of the array of inspected objects in MATLAB?. Color properties display a solid color even when the color property differs among the selected objects. Expanding the Color  property can throw a Java? exception to the command line.  ?

Summary: Unintended deletion of map graphics when working with copyobj
Description: If you use the copyobj  function to copy objects displayed with Mapping Toolbox from one axes to another and then delete the second axes (or close its figure), some of the original map content may be deleted. This is a problem in particular if the map includes polygons constructed with geoshow  or mapshow . The specific behavior is release-dependent. In R2009a through R2012a, the polygon edge may be deleted. In R2012b through R2013b, the polygon fill may be deleted.
Workaround: It's possible to correct this problem by changing settings on individual objects after they are added to a map. Contact MathWorks Technical Support if you need help with a specific case.

Description: For the System Hierarchy component, Simulink Report Generator ignores the setting for the Display peers of current system  property. The System Hierarchy component always show peers (sibling systems) of current system.

Summary: fmincon sometimes errors when there are nonlinear constraints and parallel gradient estimation.
Description: fmincon  can error under the following circumstances: The problem has nonlinear constraints The GradObj  and GradConstr  options are 'off'  (default) The UseParallel  option is 'on'  (nondefault)  The error occurs if the objective function f at the current point x evaluates to a finite real value, but a finite difference step delta leads to f(x+delta) that is Inf , NaN , or complex. The error message is: Matrix dimensions must agree.
Workaround: To avoid this bug, use serial finite differences by setting the UseParallel option to 'never': options.UseParallel = 'never';

Summary: For some df2sos filters, when cast before sum is turned off, the generated HDL code does not match the simulation results precisely.
Description: The generated HDL code does not match the simulation results exactly when the following condition exists: The Cast before Sum property is turned off (set to False) and one or more of the following occurs: There is a difference between the SectionInputFormat and the DenominatorAccumulatorFormat. Some of the coefficients are real, and some are complex.
Workaround: Setting the Cast before Sum property to True generates HDL code that matches the filter numerically.?

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Mismatch between simulation and code generation results in the Prelookup Block when breakpoint data is of type single and fraction data is of type double
Description: There is a small mismatch between simulation and code generation results, of the order of 5e-8, in the Prelookup Block when breakpoint data is of type single and fraction data is of type double. To compute the fraction, f, it uses the formula f = (i - b1)/(b2 - b1);  Here, f  is the fraction (double), i  is the input (double), and b1 , b2  are breakpoints (single). The types of operands of subtraction in the denominator are also double. The generated code conforms to the C-standard on division operation and data type casts: f = (double)(((double)i - (double)b1) / ((double)b2 - (double)b1)));  However in simulation code, Simulink performs the following cast: f = (double)(((double)i - (double)b1) / ((double)((single)b2 - (single)b1)));  This causes a difference of the order of 5e-8 between simulation and code generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In code generation, indexing into a matrix using a colon expression with unsigned endpoints and negative stride can generate wrong code.
Description: When using MATLAB?  Coder? or the MATLAB Function block in Simulink? , indexing into a matrix using a colon expression with unsigned endpoints and negative stride can generate wrong code. Here is an example: function y = foo(A,a,b,c)  y = A(uint32(a):b:uint32(c));end  Suppose that: A  is a vector of size 100. The type of a , b , and c  is double. The values of a, b , and c  are 10, -1, and 1 respectively.  The correct value of y  is a vector of 10 elements, but the generated code produces an empty vector.
Workaround: Do not use colon-based indexing with unsigned endpoints and negative stride.

Description: On some machines, when you try to save a file using the file save dialog, the dialog appears but MATLAB becomes unresponsive. You cannot interact with either the file dialog or any other MATLAB window.

Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.?

Summary: Error messagecheck_shift_count: operand_type not integer during complilation phase.
Description: During the compilation phase of a C++ verification, this error message might appear: internal error: (at end of source) check_shift_count: operand_type not integer .  It is an internal issue. For a fix, see the Workaround section.
Workaround: To workaround the issue, install the following patch on a R2012a Client software:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in C:\Polyspace (Windows) or in /usr/local/Polyspace (Linux). This folder is called <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. To see the contents of the .zip file:Windows system: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux system: Type the following in a shell: unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file.Windows system: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>             Check that binaries have appropriate 'read & execute' permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.

Summary: Last results disappear after 'Project is reloaded in the Project Browser' message
Description: This error occurs if you perform the following steps: Using the Polyspace Code Prover interface (or polyspace-code-prover.exe command), load a configuration file project with the .cfg suffix. Select the option 'Create new results folder'. Launch a new verification, answering 'Yes' to convert configuration to new .psprj file format. Open new results. The view switches to the 'Results Manager' perspective. Return to the 'Project Manager' by clicking on 'Project Manager' button.  A message appears: 'Project "my project " is reloaded in the Project Browser'. In the Project Browser window, the old .cfg file still appears and the new results have disappeared.
Workaround: Even though the new configuration and results do not appear in the Polyspace Code Prover interface, they exist in the current project folder. To see the new configuration and results in the Polyspace Code Prover interface:Close Polyspace Code Prover.Delete/move the old .cfg file from the current project folder.Reopen Polyspace Code Prover to see the new configuration and results in the Project Browser.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation may occur for a Texas Instruments? C2000? processor with the Enable portable word sizes parameter set to on
Description: The generated code may contain incorrect memset  calls that cause a mismatch between simulation and code generation results for a model that meets the following conditions: The Enable portable word sizes  parameter is set to on . The code generation target is a Texas Instruments? C2000? processor.  ?
Workaround: When generating code for a Texas Instruments? C2000? processor, clear the Enable portable word sizes parameter.

Summary: Simulating a model with inconsistent signal logging override setting triggers MATLAB assertion
Description: Simulating a model that has an inconsistent signal logging override setting generates an 'Assertion failed'  error. To check if the assertion is related to the inconsistent logging override, run the following command at the MATLAB prompt and verify that an 'Assertion failed'  error is reported. >> dlo = get_param(bdroot, 'DataLoggingOverride'),   >> dlo.assertSizeOfLogAsSpecifiedMatch  ?
Workaround: To avoid the error, assign a default Simulink.SimulationData.DataLoggingOverride object to the model. For example:dlo = Simulink.SimulationData.ModelLoggingInfo;set_param(bdroot, 'DataLoggingOverride', dlo);Then, save and simulate the model again.

However, executing the file created by MATLAB Compiler results in the following error: A 'Selected' compiler was not found. You may need to run mex -setup.

For older releases, you will need to upgrade to R2013b and follow the instructions below to apply the patch.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Once the file has been extracted, execute the following commands in MATLAB>> rehash toolboxcacheNow, run the script/program you are intending to deploy using MATLAB and verify that it is able to connect to your instrument. It should generate additional file(s) in the folder specified by executing the following code:>> sprintf('%s',[tempdir 'ICTDeploymentFiles'])A file with the name MATLABPrototypeFor<driverName>.m is generated.
