Summary: Assembly deployed from MATLAB Builder NE may crash if used in a .NET 4.5 application
Description: Using an assembly deployed with MATLAB Builder NE in a .NET 4.5 application may cause a segmentation fault crash with errors similar to the below: Faulting module name: m_interpreter.dll, version: 1.0.0.1, time stamp: 0x50fa046d Exception code: 0xc0000005  This issue can occur especially on Windows 8 or Windows Server 2012 machines as .NET 4.5 is installed by default on those operating systems.
Workaround: Turning off the MATLAB just-in-time optimization (JIT) feature can prevent the crash. This can be done by either starting up the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) with the -nojit runtime option or turning off the JIT at the beginning of the MATLAB code.For example, if you are loading the assembly in a C# application, you can turn off the JIT at runtime by specifying an assembly level attribute.using MathWorks.MATLAB.NET.Utility;[assembly: MWMCROption("-nojit")]class Program    {        static void Main(string[] args)        {            //your code        }    }}Refer to the MATLAB Builder NE documentation for more information about specifying MCR runtime options in a C# or VB application.Alternatively, you can turn off the JIT at the beginning of the MATLAB code using feature jit off command and then redeploying your assembly.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Delay and Zero-Order Hold blocks can cause memory corruption when used as a Rate Transition block.
Description: When properly configured, the Unit Delay and Zero-Order Hold blocks can be used as a Rate Transition block. However, when all of the following conditions hold good, these blocks may handle rate transitions incorrectly, thus causing data corruptions. The model is in multi-tasking mode and uses a fixed step solver. The Unit Delay block handles a slow-to-fast rate transition between input and output ports. The Zero-Order Hold block branches out from the output signals of the Unit Delay. The rate transition of the Zero-Order hold block is  fast-to-slow between input output ports.  For example, the attached model 'bug.slx' shows the above configuration with 3 discrete sample times: Ts = 1,0 Ts = 5,0 Ts = 10,0  The Unit Delay and Zero-Order Hold blocks are properly configured for handling the rate transitions between input and output ports. The C code generated and attached here, shows that the state of the Unit Delay receives values at a registered sample rate of Ts_5. It outputs new values at a registered sample rate of Ts_1: /* Model update function for TID1 */    static void bug_update1(void)  { ... bug_DWork.u0UnitDelay_DSTATE = bug_U.u0In; ... }     /* Model output function for TID0 */    static void bug_output0(void) { ... if (bug_M->Timing.RateInteraction.TID0_1) { ... bug_Y.u0Out1 = bug_DWork.u0UnitDelay_DSTATE; } ... }    However the Zero-Order Hold block incorrectly reads input values at a registered sample rate of Ts_5. This may cause data corruption because the signal is update at sample rate of Ts_1 by Unit Delay block /* Model output function for TID1 */   static void bug_output1(void) { ... if (bug_M->Timing.RateInteraction.TID1_2) { bug_B.u00ZeroOrderHold = bug_Y.u0Out1; } ... }   /* Model output function for TID2 */    static void bug_output2(void) { ... bug_Y.u00Out2 = bug_B.u00ZeroOrderHold; }   ?
Workaround: Avoid using Unit Delay and Zero-Order Hold blocks as rate transition blocks. Use the Rate Transition block from the Simulink library instead.

Summary: MATLAB crashes on exit on UNIX OS if "Close"(x) button on desktop window is clicked.
Description: In some cases running on UNIX OS platforms, closing the MATLAB session by clicking the "Close"(x) button on the desktop window title bar (upper right-hand corner in default configurations) causes segmentation violation.
Workaround: Type the quit command from the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The distance function may error for antipodal points on the sphere
Description: When the distance  function is used to calculate the distance between certain pairs of antipodal points on a sphere, the following error is thrown:Error using atan2Inputs must be real.Error in distance>greatcircledist (line 159)a = sin((lat2-lat1)/2).^2 + cos(lat1) .* cos(lat2) .* sin((lon2-lon1)/2).^2;Error in distance (line 101)    rng = greatcircledist(lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2, ellipsoid(1));
Workaround: Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Make a backup copy of the file MATLAB/toolbox/map/map/distance.m, where MATLAB refers to the output of the matlabroot command in MATLAB.Edit distance.m, inserting the following line in the greatcircledistance subfunction, immediately after the line on which the haversine a is computed:a(a > 1) = 1;Save distance.mExit and restart MATLAB.You may need to type rehash toolbox at the command prompt to update a stale toolbox cache.

Summary: Values are clipped for Vector Display in a Display Block
Description: For a vector [1:70]', the Display Block only display the number from 1 to 58.It should display all the values for vector length of 200 or less.

Summary: Cannot open Polyspace versions earlier than R2012a after installing R2013a
Description: The Polyspace preferences file for version R2013a (or later) contains a new tag acronymsetbf . This tag is not recognised by versions earlier than R2012a, which prevents the loading of preferences and stops the opening of the product.
Workaround: An additional option, -preference-folder, is provided for R2013a. With this option, you can store R2013a preferences in a separate folder and avoid altering preferences for earlier Polyspace versions.To specify this option:From the attachment, extract and place the script PVE R2013a.bat in the polyspace folder within your installation folder. The default location of this folder is C:\Program Files\PolyspaceForCandCPP_R2013a\polyspace.Manually create a folder for the preferences file. The script expects the location of the folder to be %APPDATA%\Polyspace\Prefs_13a. If you want to use another location, you must edit the script. On the last line of the script, update the value given to the option -preference-folder.From a DOS command window, run the script. Polyspace R2013a opens.

Summary: Translation fails when multiple instances of same reusable subsystem exist in a model
Description: When translating a model where two or more subsystems have identifcal function names, an error similar to this can appear in the translation log: Translation failed: The subsystems 'rtwdemo_mdlreftop_sldv_replacement2/Subsystem1' and 'rtwdemo_mdlreftop_sldv_replacement2/Subsystem' have the same function name 'ReusableFcn'. This is not legal unless the systems are identical copies of each other. Please ensure that these subsystems will have different function names by either modifying the function name of one of them or modifying the 'Maximum identifier length' parameter on the Code Generation > Symbols pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog   The error message can occur even if both instances of the subsystem are combined to a single reusable function during code generation. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Construction of objects inside loops that contain break, continue, or return can cause incorrect generated code.
Description: The code generation software can fail to detect an error condition when you: Constuct an object inside a loop that contains break,  continue , or return . Use the object after the loop completes. (To limit automatic memory management, the code generation software must not allow use of the object after the loop completes.)  If the code generation software fails to detect this error condition, the behavior of the generated code can differ from MATLAB? . This example produces incorrect results: y = 0;for j = 1:2    x = A();    x.p = j;    for i = 1:3        x.p = 0;        w = x;        if i < 3            x = A();            x.p = i;        end        y = y + w.p + x.p;        if y > 1000            return;        end    endend

Summary: Simulink.defineIntEnumType function fails before MATLAB has determined function/script precedence
Description: Simulink.defineIntEnumType  is both a package function and a help file (script).  In certain instances,such as the example on the Simulink.defineIntEnumType  help page , MATLAB executes the help file script instead of the package function.  The attempt to execute the script (help file) as a function results in an error.
Workaround: Edit your startup.m file to include the following line at the end of the file:which Simulink.defineIntEnumTypeThis forces MATLAB to give precedence to the package function over the help file script.The startup.m file is described in the startup reference page and Specifying Startup Options in the MATLAB Startup File.

Summary: Workspace browser can slow down Simulink? simulation performance when there are a large number of variables in the workspace
Description: Simulink? simulations initiated from a menu rather than by using the sim  command can run significantly more slowly when the Workspace browser is open and when there are a thousands of variables or more in the current MATLAB? workspace.
Workaround: Close the Workspace browser and the Variables editor before running the simulation. Alternatively, run the simulation using the sim command.

Summary: Model update that initiates parallel build of model reference hierarchy fails without Simulink Coder license
Description: A model update might fail with an "Invalid template makefile " error if all of the following conditions are true: The Simulink Coder product is not licensed on your MATLAB host system. Your model contains a model reference hierarchy, in which referenced models are configured in Accelerator mode. The configuration option Enable parallel model reference builds  is selected for the model.
Workaround: To work around this bug on a system with no Simulink Coder license, issue the following command for the top model, and then save the model.set_param(model,'TemplateMakefile','modelrefsim_default_tmf');

Summary: Parallel builds might fail when a parallel pool is configured with SPMD support disabled
Description: Beginning in R2013b, you can configure parallel pools (parpools) to run without single-program multiple-data (SPMD) support. This is not the default parpool behavior, but you can specify SPMD-disabled operation when you create a parpool. However, when using the SPMD-disabled pool configuration, a parallel build might fail with an error indicating that the currently open pool does not support SPMD.
Workaround: To work around this bug, create the parallel pool with default Message Passing Interface (MPI) settings, so that SPMD is supported.

Summary: MATLAB hangs sporadically using the Help browser on some Windows systems
Description: On some Windows systems, using the Help browser or Web browser sporadically causes MATLAB to hang or crash.
Workaround: For R2013a (64-bit Windows ONLY):Apply the attached patch using the following instructions.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store it in any folder.Extract the files from the .zip file to any folder. If you see Error: Access is denied when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.Exit MATLAB.Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation.Start MATLAB.Navigate to the folder that contains the extracted install_patch.m script.Run install_patch.m and follow the installation instructions. If the patch does not improve the behavior of your R2013a installation, continue to the R2012b workaround instructions below.For R2013a (32-bit Windows):Use the R2012b workaround instructions below.For R2012b:Type the following at the MATLAB command prompt and restart MATLAB. (Note that issuing this command results in a degraded appearance of documentation pages and loss of some functionality.)>> com.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.HtmlComponentFactory.setDefaultType('HTMLRENDERER');MATLAB remembers this setting across sessions.To reset the browser back to its default, type this command in MATLAB:>> com.mathworks.mlwidgets.html.HtmlComponentFactory.setDefaultType(' ');

Summary: Error during code generation when any arguments to atan2 in Stateflow are cast
Description: MATLAB throws an error when you generate code for a Stateflow chart that calls the atan2  function if any of the arguments to the function are cast in-line. For example: out1 = atan2(u1, double(u2)); The following appears near the top of the error message, before the stack trace: failed assertion in b:\matlab\src\cgir_xform\dom_core\optimization\smartinliner.cpp at line 667:Function inlining failed.
Workaround: Define a temporary variable and assign the casted value to this variable. Then use this temporary variable as the argument to atan2.

Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Polyspace Metrics displays more UNR checks than the Polyspace Environment
Description: The number of Unreachable checks (UNR) is different on Polyspace Metrics than in the Polyspace Environment. For example, Polyspace Metrics shows that N  UNR checks need to be justified. However, after downloading the results to the Polyspace User Interface, only M  UNR checks appear in the Results Manager (where M < N ). The additional UNR counted in Polyspace Metrics actually exist internally, but should not have been counted because they belong to a block that is itself unreachable.

Summary: Setting the BrowserLookUnderMasks and BrowserShowLibraryLinks parameters and preferences on a model has no effect
Description: From the MATLAB command line, using the following command has no effect in the Model Browser in the Simulink Editor: set_param('modelName', 'BrowserLookUnderMasks', 'on' ) The same is true for setting parameter BrowserShowLibraryLinks .  Also, if you set the following Simulink Display Defaults for New Models  preferences, new models ignore the values that you set for: Show masked subsystems  Show library links
Workaround: Use the context menu on the Model Browser to toggle these features.

Summary: Curve Fitting Tool crashes when changing the number of terms
Description: In the Curve Fitting app, if you change the fit type or number or terms, you might see MATLAB crash. This problem is caused by a bug in the Just-in-Time engine that causes MATLAB to crash in rare situations on Windows systems. The problem is most frequently seen when using the Curve Fitting app in the 32-bit version of MATLAB R2013a in 64-bit Windows. The bug can also occur without using the Curve Fitting app, and can impact all versions of MATLAB.
Workaround: If the problem occurs regularly, disable the Just-in-Time (JIT) mechanism in MATLAB. In the Command Window, before using the Curve Fitting Tool, type feature accel offDisabling the JIT slows down many MATLAB applications. To enable the JIT, type feature accel on

Summary: Cutting a subsystem from a library and pasting it in a model results in a subsystem with no contents
Description: If you cut a subsystem from a library and paste it in a model, the library link is maintained. However, because the library block is not present anymore (becuase it has been cut), the library link is invalid and the subsystem content is lost.
Workaround: To use a library block that you want to delete from the library:Copy the block in library.Paste the copied block into model.Break library link in model.Delete the block in the library.

Summary: xlsread cannot read from .xlsx file saved on non-English platform
Description: The xlsread  function returns an error when attempting to read a specified worksheet in a .xlsx  file last saved on a non-English platform. For example, if nonEnglish.xlsx  is a spreadsheet file last saved from a computer with a non-English locale, and Sheet1  is a worksheet in the file, then the following code: xlsread('nonEnglish.xlsx', 'Sheet1', '', 'basic')  returns the following error message: Error using xlsread (line 247)Worksheet 'Sheet1' not found.

Summary: No signal logging override settings for model reference variants
Description: Simulink gives an error when you override signal logging settings for the following configurations: Model reference Variants Model blocks that contain subsystem Variant Model blocks that contain Model reference variants

Summary: Wrong answers or error messages after recompiling a GPU MEX-file
Description: While developing GPU MEX-files, you might encounter situations where recompiling the MEX-file with the mex  command in MATLAB results in incorrect answers or in error messages reported by MATLAB. The most frequent error message reported is CUDA_ERROR_LAUNCH_FAILED .
Workaround: After every time you change and recompile the MEX-file, or issue a clear mex command, reset the GPU device. For example, if you are developing on the device whose index is 1, execute gpuDevice(1) to reset the device after compiling or running  clear mex. If this does not work, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Grouped subchart contents cannot be modified
Description: Prior to R2012b, Stateflow allowed subcharts to also be marked as "grouped" via the context menu. This is no longer supported: you can no longer modify the contents of such a subchart.
Workaround: The following steps clear the erroneous isGrouped property of the subchart contents of the subchart. You can then modify the contents of the subchart.Select the subchartUse the context menu: Group and Subchart then SubchartRepeat the previous step to toggle subcharting back on

Summary: Simulink may crash if save_system is invoked by a mask initialization callback
Description: If a model contains a block whose MaskInitialization callback invokes save_system, Simulink may crash when opening that model.

Summary: Bus Selector block dialog may be slow to open when the input bus signal has circular definition
Description: If a bus signal has circular definition via feedback loops, the Bus Selector block dialog may take a long time to open. There is a similar issue with the same root cause that causes MATLAB to hang and crash. For more information, see EBR 781812 .
Workaround: Unzip the attached P-coded file and run at the beginning of each MATLAB session (i.e. via sl_customization or run it as part of your MATLAB start-up script). Run the file as follows: oldVal = BSDiagPerf(0).

Summary: gzopen64 symbol not found in some Linux distributions.
Description: Several MEX functions in Bioinformatics Toolbox are dynamically linked to the gzopen64 symbol, therefore version 1.2.3.3 (or greater) of the ZLIB library must be available in your system. However, some Linux distributions install version 1.2.3 of the ZLIB library by default.  MathWorks supported Linux distributions that do not provide the gzopen64 are: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 5.x (/lib64/libz.so.1.2.3) Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6.x (/lib64/libz.so.1.2.3) SUSE Linux Enterprise Desktop 11.x (/lib64/libz.so.1.2.3)  Bioinformatics Toolbox MEX functions that require libz 1.2.3.3 are: bamaccessmex.mexa64  cigar2endmex.mexa64  cigar2gappedsequencemex.mexa64  cumminmaxmex.mexa64  getCountsByRangemex.mexa64  getCoverageBaseByBasemex.mexa64  getIndexByRangemex.mexa64
Workaround: Workaround 1:Ask your system administrator to upgrade the ZLIB library to version 1.2.3.3 or greater.Workaround 2:Exit MATLABPlace a temporal copy of the ZLIB binaries (or attached file) in the $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\+bioinfoprivate directory, where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine. You may place the file in any other directory where you have writting access.Create a symbolic link to the new library:cd $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\+bioinfoprivateln -s -T libz.so.1.2.5 libz.so.1 Update LD_LIBRARY_PATH:setenv LD_LIBRARY_PATH $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\+bioinfoprivate:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH

Summary: MATLAB stops responding when you access CAN Database (DBC) files
Description: Certain CAN DBC files may cause the system to stop responding when accessed using Vehicle Network Toolbox under these circumstances: DBC file was created or edited outside of the Vector CANdb++ tool DBC file contains J1939 message definitions  DBC file contains multiplexed signal definitions  ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:You need administrative privileges to save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder. To save the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder, open this Bug Report in a browser started in administrator mode.If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start WinZip in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting Run as administrator.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log:        June 19, 2014: attachment_817380_13b_win32_2014-06-16.zip: initial version, works on R2013b only        June 19, 2014: attachment_817380_13b_win64_2014-06-19.zip: initial version, works on R2013b only

Summary: MATLAB Compiler incorrectly requires non-deployable Simulink object.
Description: MATLAB Compiler and the builders automatically analyze function dependencies using depfun . At times, depfun  reports a dependency on non-deployable code, causing the compilation to fail. For example, MATLAB Compiler or one of the builders may return a message stating that a class file contains an error or can't be parsed, such as: The specified super-class 'Simulink.codemetrics.SlCodeMetrics' contains a parse error or cannot be found on MATLAB's search path, possibly shadowed by another file with the same name
Workaround: Since the performance of depfun is dependent on files on the MATLAB path, try using mcc's -N and -P switches to set the path used by the MATLAB Compiler.Alternately, experiment with changing MATLAB's path to remove the directories that contain references to the class or super-class that can't be found or parsed.

Summary: Accessing valid GPU data can sometimes generate the error 'Data no longer exists on the GPU'
Description: When the GPU has very little free memory, trying to access GPU data that is stored in a structure or object can show the error message: Data no longer exists on the GPU. rather than the correct error message: Out of memory on device.
Workaround: The error message is incorrect; the data is still valid and present on the GPU. To work around the issue, release some memory on the GPU device by clearing some gpuArray variables in MATLAB. This can allow access to the remaining GPU data.

Summary: The depfun function runs slower than in previous releases
Description: The MATLAB depfun  function runs slowly in certain cases.  Newer versions are especially slow compared to older versions.
Workaround: The performance of depfun depends on the files it finds on the MATLAB path. Try removing unnecessary directories from the path. Performance may improve.For R2014a and later, use the matlab.codetools.requiredFilesAndProducts function instead.

Summary: depfun -toponly fails to find classes called from MATLAB scripts.
Description: depfun  doesn't handle scripts well. In particular, the -toponly  switch does not work when used on a MATLAB script.
Workaround: Change the MATLAB script into a MATLAB function. depfun works much better on functions than it does on scripts. You can change the script into a function by placing a function signature at the top (the very first line) of the script file. A function signature consists of function keyword followed by a name, for example:function myfcnThe name of the first function in a file must match the name of the file. If your script file is called figure_it_out.m, then place function figure_it_out at the top of the script file to convert the script into a function.

Summary: Constant value block input sources can result in incorrect code.
Description: When the code generator reads constant data, the generator can access memory outside of the constant data storage. As a result, the generator may define constant values incorrectly in the generated code. This error is more likely to occur if the embedded target has no built-in 8 or 16-bit integer data types. The integer types are shown in the Configuration Parameters dialog box on the Hardware Implementation  pane. The problem has been observed on a 64-bit Windows host. ?
Workaround: Use 32-bit data types or a data type that is built-in to the hardware for constants or ground values.

Summary: Some blocks may not align properly in Simulink when using the alignment menus
Description: When aligning blocks in Simulink using the alignment items in the Format > Align Blocks or context menus, some blocks misalign by a pixel or two. Whether this happens or not may be affected by the specific alignment focus block, the number of ports on the block, the current position of the block relative to other the selected blocks, and the underlying 5 pixel grid.

Summary: Status bar in desktop does not immediately update after a pause function
Description: When you run the pause  function without any arguments, it causes a procedure to stop until a key is pressed. The status bar then displays Paused: Press any key  After a key is pressed, the status bar display does not update immediately. In some cases, it is not updated until the currently running function or script ends.
Workaround: As an alternative to the pause function, use the input function, which pauses the function or script and waits for input. The status bar is correctly updated as soon as the input is provided. For example, useinput('Press Enter to continue')
